2703	/-- **gap2 keystone (`lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`): basic-open section localization for
2704	a quasi-coherent sheaf on an arbitrary scheme.** For `M` quasi-coherent on `X`, an affine open `U`,
2705	and `f : Γ(X, U)`, the section restriction `Γ(M, U) → Γ(M, X.basicOpen f)` is
2706	`IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `Γ(X, U)`. Assembles the QC-pullback (Piece A,
2707	`isQuasicoherent_pullback_fromSpec`) → gap1 (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isQuasicoherent`) → the eqToHom
2708	bridge (Piece B, `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_hP1`). Project-local: closes gap2. -/
2709	theorem isLocalizedModule_basicOpen (M : X.Modules) [M.IsQuasicoherent]
2710	    {U : X.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U) (f : Γ(X, U))
2711	    [Module Γ(X, U) Γ(M, X.basicOpen f)]
2712	    [IsScalarTower Γ(X, U) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) Γ(M, X.basicOpen f)] :
2713	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (restrictBasicOpenₗ M f) :=
2714	  haveI := isQuasicoherent_pullback_fromSpec M hU
2715	  isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_hP1 M hU
2716	    (isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isQuasicoherent ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj M)) f
2717	
2718	/-- **Per-affine coherence of the annihilator family** (the `map_ideal_basicOpen` content for
2719	`def:modules_annihilator`, `lem:modules_annihilator_ideal`). For a quasi-coherent `F`, an affine
2720	open `V`, and `f : Γ(X, V)` with `Γ(F, V)` finitely generated over `Γ(X, V)`, the module annihilator
2721	`Ann_{Γ(X,V)}(Γ(F,V))` maps, under the basic-open restriction `Γ(X, V) → Γ(X, D(f))`, onto
2722	`Ann_{Γ(X,D(f))}(Γ(F,D(f)))`. This is the localized-annihilator identity
2723	(`Module.annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map`, `lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map`) transported
2724	across the quasi-coherent basic-open section localization (gap2, `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`,
2725	`lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`). It is exactly the structure field
2726	`Scheme.IdealSheafData.map_ideal_basicOpen` for the annihilator data. Project-local because
2727	`annihilator` is. -/
2728	lemma annihilator_map_basicOpen (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
2729	    (V : X.affineOpens) (f : Γ(X, V.1)) [Module.Finite Γ(X, V.1) Γ(F, V.1)] :
2730	    (Module.annihilator Γ(X, V.1) Γ(F, V.1)).map
2731	        (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)).op).hom
2732	      = Module.annihilator Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) Γ(F, X.basicOpen f) := by
2733	  haveI := V.2.isLocalization_basicOpen f
2734	  letI : Module Γ(X, V.1) Γ(F, X.basicOpen f) :=
2735	    Module.compHom _ (algebraMap Γ(X, V.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f))
2736	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(X, V.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) Γ(F, X.basicOpen f) :=
2737	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun _ _ => rfl)
2738	  haveI := isLocalizedModule_basicOpen F V.2 f
2739	  exact (Module.annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map (Submonoid.powers f)
2740	    (restrictBasicOpenₗ F f)).symm
2741	
2742	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
2743	/-- **The annihilator ideal sheaf agrees with the module annihilator on affine opens**
2744	(`lem:modules_annihilator_ideal`). For a quasi-coherent `F` whose section module `Γ(F, V)` is
2745	finitely generated over `Γ(X, V)` on every affine open `V` (the affine-local finiteness supplied,
2746	for a finite-type `F`, by the G1 base case `lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections`), the section over
2747	an affine open `U` of the annihilator ideal sheaf equals the module annihilator of the sections:
2748	`(annihilator F).ideal U = Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))`.
2749	
2750	The forward inclusion `≤` is `annihilator_ideal_le` (always available from `ofIdeals`). The reverse
2751	inclusion is genuinely *global*: `annihilator F = ofIdeals (V ↦ Ann_{Γ(X,V)}(Γ(F,V)))` is the
2752	**largest** ideal sheaf contained in the annihilator family, and its value at `U` reaches the full
2753	`Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))` precisely when that family is itself a coherent ideal sheaf — i.e. when it
2754	satisfies `IdealSheafData.map_ideal_basicOpen` at *every* affine open. That coherence is
2755	`annihilator_map_basicOpen`, which needs finite generation of `Γ(F, V)` at each `V`; hence the
2756	`hfin` hypothesis. With it the family assembles into an honest `IdealSheafData I` whose `ideal` is
2757	the annihilator family, and `IdealSheafData.ofIdeals_ideal` gives `ofIdeals I.ideal = I`, whence the
2758	claim at every `U` at once. This mirrors Mathlib's `Scheme.Hom.ker_apply`, which likewise builds the
2759	full ideal sheaf and reads off the affine value (using `QuasiCompact f` as its global hypothesis).
2760	Project-local because `annihilator` is. -/
2761	theorem annihilator_ideal (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
2762	    (hfin : ∀ V : X.affineOpens, Module.Finite Γ(X, V.1) Γ(F, V.1)) (U : X.affineOpens) :
2763	    (annihilator F).ideal U = Module.annihilator Γ(X, U.1) Γ(F, U.1) := by
2764	  let I : X.IdealSheafData :=
2765	    ⟨fun V => Module.annihilator Γ(X, V.1) Γ(F, V.1), ?_, _, rfl⟩
2766	  · exact congr($(IdealSheafData.ofIdeals_ideal I).ideal U)
2767	  · intro V f
2768	    haveI := hfin V
2769	    exact annihilator_map_basicOpen F V f
2770	
2771	end Scheme.Modules

/-- **B1.0 — localization commutes with base tensor**
(`lem:gf_localizedModule_baseChange_tensor_comm`).

Let `R → B` be a ring map, `T ⊆ B` a submonoid, `N` a `B`-module (hence an `R`-module
through the tower `R → B → N`), and `K` an `R`-module.  Then localising `N ⊗[R] K` at the
source submonoid `T` is the same as localising `N` and then tensoring with `K`:
`LocalizedModule T (N ⊗[R] K) ≃ₗ[R] (LocalizedModule T N) ⊗[R] K`.

The construction uses that `(LocalizedModule T N) ⊗[R] K` is itself a localization of
`N ⊗[R] K` at `T` (`IsLocalizedModule.rTensor`, the base-change identity
`T⁻¹M ⊗[R] N = T⁻¹(M ⊗[R] N)`) and then transports the canonical localization through
uniqueness of localizations (`IsLocalizedModule.iso`).  It is natural in `K` by construction
(it is the `iso` of a natural transformation).  Project-local: pins the blueprint sub-lemma
feeding `gf_flat_localizedModule_sameBase`. -/
noncomputable def gf_localizedModule_baseChange_tensor_comm {R B N : Type*} [CommRing R]
    [CommRing B] [Algebra R B] [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [Module B N] [IsScalarTower R B N]
    (T : Submonoid B) (K : Type*) [AddCommGroup K] [Module R K] :
    LocalizedModule T (TensorProduct R N K) ≃ₗ[R] TensorProduct R (LocalizedModule T N) K :=
  (IsLocalizedModule.iso T
    (AlgebraTensorModule.rTensor R K (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap T N))).restrictScalars R

/-- **B1 — localizing the source ring preserves flatness over the base**
(`lem:gf_flat_localizedModule_sameBase`).

Let `R → B → N` be a scalar tower with `N` a `B`-module, and `T ⊆ B` a submonoid of the
*source* ring `B`.  Viewing `N` as an `R`-module through the tower, if `N` is flat over `R`
then `LocalizedModule T N` is again flat over `R`.

Unlike Mathlib's `Module.Flat.localizedModule` / `Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule`, which
localize over a submonoid of the *base* `R`, here the submonoid lives in the source `B` while
the base `R` is untouched.  Proof: flatness is checked by `Module.Flat.iff_lTensor_injectiveₛ`;
for an `R`-submodule `N₀ ≤ P` the map `(N₀.subtype).lTensor (LocalizedModule T N)` is, via the
base-tensor/localization commutation `IsLocalizedModule.map_lTensor`
(cf. `gf_localizedModule_baseChange_tensor_comm`), the `T`-localization of
`(N₀.subtype).lTensor N`; the latter is injective because `N` is `R`-flat
(`Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap`) and `T`-localization preserves
injectivity (`IsLocalizedModule.map_injective`, exactness of localization).  Project-local: the
single genuine Mathlib gap of the flat-locality assembly. -/
theorem gf_flat_localizedModule_sameBase {R B N : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing B]
    [Algebra R B] [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [Module B N] [IsScalarTower R B N]
    (T : Submonoid B) [Module.Flat R N] :
    Module.Flat R (LocalizedModule T N) := by
  rw [Module.Flat.iff_lTensor_injectiveₛ]
  intro P _ _ N₀
  have key := IsLocalizedModule.map_lTensor (R := R) (S := T)
      (M := N) (M' := LocalizedModule T N) (g := LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap T N)
      (N := N₀) (P := P) (f := N₀.subtype)
  rw [← AlgebraTensorModule.coe_lTensor (A := B), ← key]
  apply IsLocalizedModule.map_injective
  rw [AlgebraTensorModule.coe_lTensor]
  exact Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap _ Subtype.val_injective

/-- **Generic flatness theorem** (Nitsure §4 / Stacks ?).

# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean

## Summary

  - `AlgebraicGeometry.gf_localizedModule_baseChange_tensor_comm` (B1.0) — line ~2765

  cross-chart basic-open plumbing (see "Why I stopped"); the algebra gap is now closed.

  line ~2925). No new sorries introduced.
- **Build:** `lake build AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.FlatteningStratification` succeeds (GREEN), 8318 jobs,
  only the expected `genericFlatness` `sorry` warning.

## gf_localizedModule_baseChange_tensor_comm (B1.0) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Statement:** `LocalizedModule T (N ⊗[R] K) ≃ₗ[R] (LocalizedModule T N) ⊗[R] K` for `R → B`,
  `T : Submonoid B`, `N` a `B`-module (`R`-module via tower), `K` an `R`-module.
- **Approach:** `(LocalizedModule T N) ⊗[R] K` is itself a `T`-localization of `N ⊗[R] K` via the
  Mathlib **instance** `IsLocalizedModule.rTensor` (the base-change identity `T⁻¹M ⊗[R] N = T⁻¹(M ⊗[R] N)`,
  `Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.BaseChange`). Transport the canonical localization through
  `IsLocalizedModule.iso` (uniqueness of localizations) and `.restrictScalars R`.
- **Relies on:** `IsLocalizedModule.rTensor` (instance), `IsLocalizedModule.iso`,
  `TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.rTensor`, `LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap`.
- `#print axioms` = `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.

## gf_flat_localizedModule_sameBase (B1) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Statement:** tower `R → B → N`, `T : Submonoid B`, `[Module.Flat R N]` ⟹
  `Module.Flat R (LocalizedModule T N)`. (Localizing the **source** ring `B` preserves flatness over
  the fixed **base** `R`. The existing `Module.Flat.localizedModule` / `.of_isLocalizedModule` localize
  the *base* — wrong direction — so this was the one genuine Mathlib gap flagged in PROGRESS/blueprint.)
- **Approach:** `Module.Flat.iff_lTensor_injectiveₛ` (universe-of-`R` submodule test, no `Small` side
  condition). For `N₀ ≤ P`, the map `(N₀.subtype).lTensor (LocalizedModule T N)` is, by
  `IsLocalizedModule.map_lTensor` + `AlgebraTensorModule.coe_lTensor`, the `T`-localization of
  `(N₀.subtype).lTensor N`. The latter is injective by `Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap`
  (`N` flat), and `T`-localization preserves injectivity by `IsLocalizedModule.map_injective`
  (exactness of localization). Mirrors Mathlib's `IsLocalization.flat` / `Module.flat_of_isLocalized_span`
  proof style.
- **Key insight:** the canonical `Module R (LocalizedModule T N)` and `IsScalarTower R B (LocalizedModule T N)`
  instances already exist in Mathlib (restrict-scalars through the tower) — do NOT supply your own (creates a
  diamond and `IsScalarTower` synthesis fails). Verified by `infer_instance`.
- **Relies on:** `Module.Flat.iff_lTensor_injectiveₛ`, `IsLocalizedModule.map_lTensor`,
  `IsLocalizedModule.map_injective`, `TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.coe_lTensor`,
  `Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap`.
- `#print axioms` = `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.

## gf_section_localization_flat_descent (B2) — NOT ADDED (geometric)
- **Why not:** B2 statement (1) (`Γ(F,W) → Γ(F,D(g))` is `IsLocalizedModule (powers g)`) is *literally*
  `Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen` (QuotScheme.lean, DONE) applied with ambient affine `W`.
  The novel content is statement (2): **chart-independence** — the same group `Γ(F,D(g))` is also the
  localization of `M_j = Γ(F,W_j)` at the image `ḡ ∈ Γ(X,W_j)`. Both legs are `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`
  in the two charts, but connecting them requires matching `X.basicOpen g` (computed via `Γ(X,W)`) with
  `X.basicOpen ḡ` (computed via `Γ(X,W_j)`) as the SAME open `X.Opens` — the cross-chart basic-open
  identity (`Scheme.basicOpen` compatibility under restriction). That geometric matching, plus fixing the
  full assembly variable context (charts `W_j`, element `f`, base comparison `Γ(S,U) ⊥ A_f`), is a
  multi-step scheme-theory build, not a quick instantiation.
- **Next step:** prove the cross-chart lemma `X.basicOpen (g) = X.basicOpen (res_{W→W∩W_j} g)` (or the
  `IsLocalizedModule` transport across the two charts), then assemble B2 from the two
  `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen` legs + `IsLocalizedModule.iso` uniqueness.

## gf_flat_locality_assembly + genericFlatness — NOT ADDED (geometric)
- **Why not:** with B1 + B1.0 in hand, the assembly's *algebraic* core reduces to
  `Module.flat_of_isLocalized_span` (Mathlib, `lem:mathlib_flat_of_isLocalized_span`) over the source ring
  `B = Γ(X,W)`, fed by B1 + base-localization + the per-patch freeness `(M_j)_f`. The **remaining**
  obligations are entirely geometric (now documented in the updated `genericFlatness` GAP-G3 comment):
  (i) finite affine cover of `p⁻¹(U₀)` with `B_j` finite-type over `A` (quasi-compactness of `p`);
  (ii) covering arbitrary affine `W ≤ p⁻¹U` by basic opens `D(g) ⊆ W ∩ W_j` whose `g`'s span the unit
  ideal of `Γ(X,W)` (quasi-compactness of `W`); (iii) the cross-chart identification of B2.
- These were left absent rather than stubbed; no `sorry` added. The existing `genericFlatness` `sorry`
  is unchanged (its signature is advisory-frozen; only the GAP-G3 comment was updated to record that the
  algebra core is now closed).

## Needs blueprint entry
- **None for the two new declarations.** Both already have blueprint blocks with `\lean{}` hints in
  `chapters/Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`:
  - `lem:gf_localizedModule_baseChange_tensor_comm` → `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_localizedModule_baseChange_tensor_comm}`
  - `lem:gf_flat_localizedModule_sameBase` → `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_flat_localizedModule_sameBase}`
  These are now formalized (statement + closed proof); the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase should add
  `\leanok` to both statement and proof blocks. No new `lean_aux` nodes were introduced.

## Why I stopped
- **Real progress:** 2 axiom-clean declarations added —
  `gf_localizedModule_baseChange_tensor_comm` (B1.0, line ~2765) and
  `gf_flat_localizedModule_sameBase` (B1, line ~2790). B1 is the single genuine Mathlib gap that
  PROGRESS/blueprint flagged for the whole source-span descent; it is now closed.
- **Partial on the lane goal:** `genericFlatness` is NOT closed. The blocker is precisely specified and is
  **geometric, not algebraic**: the cross-chart basic-open identification (B2 statement (2)) and the
  quasi-compactness covering arguments (i)/(ii). Each is a distinct scheme-theory build on top of the now-
  available `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen` (DONE) and B1.
- **No informal agent:** no LLM API key in env (per PROGRESS "No LLM API key in env"); not invoked.
- **Approaches considered and correctly deferred (not skipped silently):** a purely-algebraic
  `gf_flat_locality_assembly` intermediate — verified this collapses to Mathlib's
  `Module.flat_of_isLocalized_span` (already the blueprint's `lem:mathlib_flat_of_isLocalized_span`), so
  there is no algebraic helper left to add; the remaining work is the geometric plumbing above. Attempting
  the cross-chart geometry within the remaining budget was judged high-risk-of-churn for a ~100s/build
  file; handed off with a precise decomposition instead.

  - Editing another agent's `.lean` file.
  - Removing an existing public declaration that other files import.
  - Changing the type of an existing declaration in a way that would break other files.

  Everything else — including trying a strategy the planner didn't explicitly endorse — is your call to make and attempt now.

## When to stop

Stop only when progress is blocked by a specific, named obstacle in one of these categories:

1. A genuinely missing Mathlib ingredient — named precisely, attempted via informal agent, no workaround found.
2. A definition or signature that requires another prover's output — name the specific declaration and file.
3. A proof that requires fundamental restructuring of a protected or cross-file definition — name it.

"It seems hard", "the proof is complex", "I couldn't find the right lemma", or "the planner should weigh in on this approach" are NOT valid stop reasons. Document the specific obstacle and keep trying. Only stop when you can write: "I tried approaches X, Y, Z; each fails because [specific mathematical reason]; the informal agent suggested [route] which requires [specific missing ingredient]."

**Specifically banned stop phrases:**
- "I'll wait for the planner to decide."
- "This strategy should be confirmed by the next iteration."
- "I'll defer this choice to the planner."
- "The planner may want to reconsider the approach."

If you find yourself writing any of these: delete the sentence, pick the most promising route, and attempt it. The planner will correct the choice next iter if it was wrong — a failed attempt costs nothing compared to an idle session.

## Completion criteria

Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:

1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
2. Zero axioms introduced.
3. The file compiles cleanly.

## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof

When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature**. Three patterns are banned:

- **Reflexive-iso placeholder** — replacing `X ≅ Y` with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`.
- **`Classical.choice` around an explicit witness** — dissolving `Type` vs `Prop` friction without actually constructing the witness.
- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`** — discards the declaration post-elaboration, breaking blueprint cross-references.

**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, ship the typed `sorry` instead.

## When infrastructure is missing

Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before filing "Infrastructure missing":

1. **Use the informal agent** if an API key is available (check `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` first): call `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` with "Prove [goal] using only current Mathlib." Formalize whatever it suggests. If no key is set, skip to step 2.
2. **If the missing ingredient is ≤~100 LOC**: write it as a project-local helper — typed signature plus a genuine proof attempt. A partial proof body is better than a documented gap.
3. **Only after steps 1–2 are exhausted**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` with what you tried, why it failed, and the precise statement of the missing ingredient for the plan agent to assign in `mathlib-build` mode.

"Infrastructure missing" as a verdict requires: informal agent called (or key unavailable, documented), local helper attempted if in scope. "I don't see how" is not evidence — a failed attempt with `sorry` at the stuck step IS evidence.

When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.

**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.

## Proof style

- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is display-level only. Default to `change` when in doubt.

## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md

The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:

- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.

## LSP MCP tools

The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (`mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal`, `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`, etc.). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, …) — same tools, never shell binaries.

- Always invoke through your tool-call interface.
- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` — there is no such shell command.
- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If `success: false`, retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.

## Search protocol

1. `lean_local_search` first.
2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.

## Tooling traps

- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading. Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors. Rely on `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.

## Logging

Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):

```markdown
# Algebra/WLocal.lean

## wLocal_iff (line 45)
### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance
- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing

### Attempt 2
- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
- **Result:** RESOLVED
- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
```

One section per theorem/lemma. Each attempt: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results.

## End-of-session handoff

**Before declaring done, run this self-review:**

1. Did I attempt every approach I wrote down as a comment, TODO, or "possible route"? If not, go back and attempt them.
2. Are there other open `sorry`s in my file I could attempt with the tools and lemmas I just developed?
3. Did I call the informal agent (or document that no key was available) before filing "Infrastructure missing"?
4. Is there any approach I thought of but skipped because "it would take too long" or "the planner should decide"? If it's in my file's scope, attempt it.

Only proceed to write the task result when all four answers are "yes" or "not applicable."

Before writing results:

1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles.
3. **Write a `## Summary` section** stating: sorry count before → after; exact names of sorries closed; exact names of sorries still open and why; whether you attempted adjacent sorries beyond the assigned ones.
4. **Write a `## Why I stopped` section** — be brutally honest; the planner reads this. **Only Lean code changes count as progress** — a sorry closed, a helper lemma that compiles, a partial tactic block at a specific stuck point. Comments, docstrings, TODO notes, and task result prose do NOT count. If you wrote approaches in comments that you didn't attempt, say so explicitly:
   - `Real progress`: closed N sorries — name each one. State the sorry count before and after.
   - `Partial progress`: made measurable code progress (decomposed into sub-lemmas with proof bodies, closed one branch of a case split) but did not fully close. Name the specific advance and the specific remaining blocker. Decomposing a `sorry` into N named `sorry`s with only comments in their bodies is NOT partial progress unless the bodies contain genuine proof attempts.
   - `Cosmetics only`: changed formatting/comments/style with no proof progress — say so explicitly. **Renaming a `sorry` to a named helper lemma, adding a docstring, or extracting it to a separate declaration with a `sorry` body is `Cosmetics only` unless the body contains a genuine proof attempt.**
   - `Approaches written but not attempted`: you identified routes (in comments, TODOs, or log prose) but did not attempt them — name each one and why you stopped before trying. This is a valid but weak verdict; the planner will re-assign with a directive to attempt them.
   - `Avoided the goal`: attempted something adjacent but not the assigned target — explain why and what you tried instead.
   - `Infrastructure missing`: a specific Mathlib gap was the blocker — name it precisely, confirm the informal agent was called (or key unavailable), describe what it suggested, why it failed, and state the exact missing ingredient statement.
   - `Directive not followed`: explain which part of the planner's directive you deviated from and why.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
128	
129	### Prover Agent
130	
131	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
132	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
133	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
134	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
135	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
136	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
137	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
138	
139	### Subagents
140	
141	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
142	
143	### Review Agent
144	
145	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
146	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
147	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
148	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
149	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
150	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
151	
152	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
153	
154	These run automatically between phases:
155	
156	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
157	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
158	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
159

1	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GrassmannianCells
2	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotScheme
3	
4	/-!
5	# The tautological quotient and the universal property of `Gr(r,d)`
6	
7	This file adds, on top of the Grassmannian scheme `Gr(d,r)` built in
8	`GrassmannianCells.lean`, the tautological rank-`d` quotient
9	`u : O^r ↠ U` and the universal property making `Gr(d,r)` the fine moduli
10	space of rank-`d` locally free quotients of `O^r`.
11	
12	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianQuot.tex` (Nitsure §1).
13	-/
14	
15	universe u
16	
17	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite
18	
19	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian
20	
21	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — scalar endomorphisms of the structure sheaf
22	
23	To realise a matrix of regular functions as a morphism of free sheaves of modules we
24	need to turn a global section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)` of the structure sheaf into a scalar
25	endomorphism of `O_X` (= `SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf`). On the affine chart
26	`U^I = Spec R^I` the matrix entries of the universal matrix `X^I` live in `R^I`, and we
27	inject them into the global sections via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`.
28	
29	These helpers are project-local: Mathlib has no ready-made "matrix ↦ morphism of free
30	sheaves" primitive. -/
31	
32	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
33	
34	/-- The global section of the structure sheaf `O_X` (as a sheaf of modules over itself)
35	determined by a section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)` over the whole space, by restriction to every open.
36	Project-local helper for building scalar endomorphisms. -/
37	noncomputable def globalUnitSection (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
38	    (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).sections :=
39	  PresheafOfModules.sectionsMk
40	    (fun Y => (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y))
41	    (by
42	      intro Y Z f
43	      change (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f) (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a)
44	        = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a
45	      rw [← CategoryTheory.comp_apply, ← X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map_comp]
46	      congr 1)
47	
48	/-- The scalar endomorphism of `O_X` given by a global section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)`:
49	multiplication by `a`. Project-local helper. -/
50	noncomputable def scalarEnd (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
51	    SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
52	  (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv.symm (globalUnitSection a)
53	
54	/-- `scalarEnd 1` is the identity endomorphism of `O_X`. Project-local helper for
55	identifying the diagonal entries of the chart quotient. -/
56	lemma scalarEnd_one : scalarEnd (1 : Γ(X, ⊤)) = 𝟙 (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) := by
57	  rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
58	  ext Y
59	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (1 : Γ(X, ⊤))
60	      = (SheafOfModules.Hom.val (𝟙 (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf))).app Y
61	          (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
62	  rw [SheafOfModules.id_val, PresheafOfModules.id_app]
63	  exact map_one _
64	
65	/-- `scalarEnd 0` is the zero endomorphism of `O_X`. Project-local helper for identifying
66	the off-diagonal entries of the chart quotient. -/
67	lemma scalarEnd_zero : scalarEnd (0 : Γ(X, ⊤)) = 0 := by
68	  rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
69	  ext Y
70	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (0 : Γ(X, ⊤))
71	      = (SheafOfModules.Hom.val
72	          (0 : SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).app Y
73	          (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
74	  refine (map_zero _).trans ?_
75	  rfl
76	
77	/-! ## The tautological quotient on the charts -/
78	
79	/-- The **chart quotient** `u^I : O_{U^I}^r → O_{U^I}^d` (`def:gr_chart_quotient`):
80	left multiplication by the universal matrix `X^I` (`universalMatrix`). It is realised as
81	the morphism of free sheaves of modules whose matrix of components, in the standard bases
82	`(e_{i'})_{i' : Fin r}` and `(e_p)_{p : Fin d}`, is the universal matrix `X^I` injected
83	into the structure sheaf via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. Since the `I`-minor of `X^I` is the
84	identity, `u^I` is a split surjection onto the free rank-`d` sheaf.
85	
86	Project-local: Mathlib has no "matrix ↦ morphism of free sheaves" primitive. -/
87	noncomputable def chartQuotientMap (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
88	    SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶
89	      SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) :=
90	  let A := CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
91	  let R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf
92	  haveI : HasFiniteBiproducts (SheafOfModules R) :=
93	    HasFiniteBiproducts.of_hasFiniteProducts
94	  let M : ∀ (_ : Fin r) (_ : Fin d), SheafOfModules.unit R ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit R :=
95	    fun i' p => scalarEnd ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv.hom ((universalMatrix d r I hI) p i'))
96	  (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin r => SheafOfModules.unit R)).symm.hom ≫
97	    biproduct.matrix M ≫
98	    (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit R)).hom
99	
100	/-- The chart quotient `u^I` sends the `(I.orderIsoOfFin k)`-th basis section of
101	`O_{U^I}^r` to the `k`-th basis section of `O_{U^I}^d`. Project-local: the column-`I`
102	restriction of `u^I` is the identity, the matrix-level content of `lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi`. -/
103	private lemma chartQuotientMap_ιFree (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
104	    (k : Fin d) :
105	    SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf)
106	        ((I.orderIsoOfFin hI k : Fin r)) ≫ chartQuotientMap d r I hI
107	      = SheafOfModules.ιFree k := by
108	  set A := CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) with hA
109	  set S := AlgebraicGeometry.Spec A with hS
110	  haveI : HasFiniteBiproducts (SheafOfModules S.ringCatSheaf) :=
111	    HasFiniteBiproducts.of_hasFiniteProducts
112	  change SheafOfModules.ιFree (↑((I.orderIsoOfFin hI) k)) ≫
113	      ((biproduct.isoCoproduct
114	            (fun _ : Fin r => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).symm.hom ≫
115	        biproduct.matrix (fun (i' : Fin r) (p : Fin d) => scalarEnd
116	          ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv.hom (universalMatrix d r I hI p i'))) ≫
117	        (biproduct.isoCoproduct
118	            (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).hom)
119	      = SheafOfModules.ιFree k
120	  rw [Iso.symm_hom, SheafOfModules.ιFree, biproduct.isoCoproduct_inv]
121	  erw [Sigma.ι_desc_assoc]
122	  rw [biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.isoCoproduct_hom]
123	  have h1 : (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv) (1 : A) = 1 := map_one _
124	  have h0 : (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv) (0 : A) = 0 := map_zero _
125	  have hsub := universalMatrix_submatrix_self d r I hI
126	  have lift_eq :
127	      (biproduct.lift fun p : Fin d => scalarEnd
128	          ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv.hom (universalMatrix d r I hI p (↑((I.orderIsoOfFin hI) k)))))
129	        = biproduct.ι (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) k := by
130	    refine biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun p => ?_)
131	    rw [biproduct.lift_π]
132	    have hentry : universalMatrix d r I hI p (↑((I.orderIsoOfFin hI) k))
133	        = (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) A) p k :=
134	      congrFun (congrFun hsub p) k
135	    rw [hentry, Matrix.one_apply]
136	    by_cases hpk : p = k
137	    · rw [if_pos hpk, h1, scalarEnd_one, hpk, biproduct.ι_π_self]
138	    · rw [if_neg hpk, h0, scalarEnd_zero, biproduct.ι_π_ne _ (Ne.symm hpk)]
139	  rw [lift_eq, biproduct.ι_desc]
140	  rfl
141	
142	/-- **The chart quotient is an epimorphism** (`lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi`): `u^I` is split
143	by the coordinate inclusion `s_I` of the `I`-indexed columns, hence is a (split) epimorphism
144	of sheaves of modules. -/
145	lemma chartQuotientMap_epi (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
146	    Epi (chartQuotientMap d r I hI) := by
147	  have hsplit : SheafOfModules.freeMap (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf)
148	        (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) ≫ chartQuotientMap d r I hI
149	      = 𝟙 (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) := by
150	    refine Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext (SheafOfModules.isColimitFreeCofan (Fin d)) _ _ (fun k => ?_)
151	    have key : SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) k ≫
152	          (SheafOfModules.freeMap (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) ≫
153	            chartQuotientMap d r I hI)
154	        = SheafOfModules.ιFree k :=
155	      (SheafOfModules.ιFree_freeMap_assoc (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) k
156	        (chartQuotientMap d r I hI)).trans (chartQuotientMap_ιFree d r I hI k)
157	    exact key.trans (Category.comp_id _).symm
158	  exact (IsSplitEpi.mk' ⟨_, hsplit⟩).epi
159	
160	end AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian
161	
162	/-! ## Gluing a sheaf of modules along a scheme glue datum
163	
164	`Scheme.Modules.glue` descends a sheaf of modules from per-chart data plus a transition
165	cocycle over a `Scheme.GlueData`. Mathlib carries no turn-key module descent over a
166	scheme glue datum (confirmed), so this is an Archon-original construction. The
167	construction path (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue`): restrict to a chart through the
168	open-immersion pullback equivalence (`Scheme.Modules.overRestrictPullbackIso`) and glue
169	the local sections by locality of sections (`existsUnique_gluing'`).
170	
171	NOTE (scaffold): the body and the module-cocycle hypotheses on `g` are still to be filled;
172	the transition data `g` (per-overlap pullback isos) is recorded in the signature, the
173	multiplicative cocycle conditions remain to be added before the construction is closed. -/
174	
175	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
176	
177	/-! ### Base-change transport of a transition isomorphism to a triple overlap
178	
179	To state the triple-overlap multiplicativity (C2) of a module descent datum we must
180	transport each transition isomorphism `g_ij`, living on the overlap `V_ij`, to the common
181	triple overlap `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik`. The transport pulls `g_ij` back along a
182	projection `p : V_ijk ⟶ V_ij` and reassociates the iterated pullbacks via the pseudofunctor
183	comparison `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`. The three scheme-level `glueData_bridge_*`
184	identities below (consequences of `t_fac`, `pullback.condition` and `cocycle`) line up the
185	endpoints of the three transports so that the cocycle equation is well typed. -/
186	
187	/-- **Base-change transport of a transition isomorphism along a morphism**
188	(`lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport`). Given a transition isomorphism
189	`g : a^*Mᵢ ≅ b^*Mⱼ` over `V` and a morphism `p : W ⟶ V`, transport it to `W` as an
190	isomorphism `(p ≫ a)^*Mᵢ ≅ (p ≫ b)^*Mⱼ`, by pulling `g` back along `p` and reassociating
191	the iterated pullbacks through `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`.
192	
193	Project-local: this is the pullback-pseudofunctor packaging that lets the three transition
194	isomorphisms attached to a triple of charts be compared on a single triple overlap; Mathlib
195	has no descent-of-modules-over-a-scheme-glue-datum API. -/
196	noncomputable def pullbackBaseChangeTransport {W V : Scheme.{u}} (p : W ⟶ V)
197	    {Yi Yj : Scheme.{u}} (a : V ⟶ Yi) (b : V ⟶ Yj)
198	    {Mi : Yi.Modules} {Mj : Yj.Modules}
199	    (g : (Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj Mi ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj Mj) :
200	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (p ≫ a)).obj Mi ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback (p ≫ b)).obj Mj :=
201	  (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p a).symm.app Mi ≪≫
202	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).mapIso g ≪≫
203	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p b).app Mj
204	
205	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (source): on `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik` the two projections to
206	`V_ij` and `V_ik` followed by the overlap immersions `f_ij`, `f_ik` agree as morphisms to
207	`U_i`. This is the pullback condition; it identifies the sources of the `ij`- and
208	`ik`-transports. Project-local helper for the module cocycle (C2). -/
209	theorem glueData_bridge_src (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
210	    pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.f i j
211	      = pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.f i k := pullback.condition
212	
213	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (middle): the `ij`-transition's target leg
214	`p^{ij} ≫ (t_ij ≫ f_ji)` to `U_j` coincides with the `jk`-transition's source leg
215	`(t'_ijk ≫ p^{jk}) ≫ f_jk`. Follows from `t_fac` and the pullback condition; it identifies
216	the target of the `ij`-transport with the source of the `jk`-transport. Project-local helper
217	for the module cocycle (C2). -/
218	theorem glueData_bridge_mid (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
219	    pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)
220	      = (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i)) ≫ D.f j k := by
221	  rw [Category.assoc, pullback.condition, ← Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc, D.t_fac i j k,
222	    Category.assoc]
223	
224	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (target): the `jk`-transition's target leg
225	`(t'_ijk ≫ p^{jk}) ≫ (t_jk ≫ f_kj)` to `U_k` coincides with the `ik`-transition's target
226	leg `p^{ik} ≫ (t_ik ≫ f_ki)`. This is the heart of the cocycle, derived from `t_fac`, the
227	pullback condition, `t_inv` and `cocycle`; it identifies the target of the composite
228	`jk`-after-`ij` transport with the target of the `ik`-transport. Project-local helper for
229	the module cocycle (C2). -/
230	theorem glueData_bridge_tgt (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
231	    (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i)) ≫ (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j)
232	      = pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) := by
233	  have key : pullback.fst (D.f k i) (D.f k j) ≫ D.f k i
234	      = D.t' k i j ≫ pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.t i k ≫ D.f k i := by
235	    rw [D.t_fac_assoc k i j, ← Category.assoc (D.t k i) (D.t i k), D.t_inv, Category.id_comp]
236	  rw [Category.assoc, ← D.t_fac_assoc j k i,
237	    ← @pullback.condition _ _ _ _ _ (D.f k i) (D.f k j) _, key, D.cocycle_assoc i j k]
238	
239	/-- **Gluing a sheaf of modules along an open cover given by a scheme glue datum**
240	(`def:scheme_modules_glue`). From a glue datum `D`, per-chart sheaves of modules `M i`,
241	and transition isomorphisms `g i j` comparing the two charts' sheaves over the overlap
242	`V (i,j)` (after pullback), produces a glued sheaf of `O_{D.glued}`-modules.
243	
244	Project-local: Mathlib has no module descent over a scheme glue datum. -/
245	noncomputable def glue (D : Scheme.GlueData)
246	    (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)
247	    (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
248	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j))
249	    -- (C1) self-identity: over the diagonal overlap `V (i,i)` (where `t i i = 𝟙`) the
250	    -- transition isomorphism is the identity, i.e. the canonical isomorphism induced by
251	    -- `f i i = t i i ≫ f i i` (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue` (C1)).
252	    (_hC1 : ∀ i, g i i = eqToIso (congrArg (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (M i))
253	        (show D.f i i = D.t i i ≫ D.f i i by rw [D.t_id i, Category.id_comp])))
254	    -- (C2) triple-overlap multiplicativity: over each triple overlap
255	    -- `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik` the base-change transports
256	    -- (`pullbackBaseChangeTransport`) of the three transition isomorphisms `g_ij`, `g_jk`,
257	    -- `g_ik` satisfy `ĝ_jk ∘ ĝ_ij = ĝ_ik`. The three `glueData_bridge_*` identities, applied
258	    -- through `pullbackCongr`, line up the endpoints so the equation is well typed
259	    -- (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue` (C2), `lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport`).
260	    (_hC2 : ∀ i j k,
261	        pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
262	            (D.f i j) (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (g i j) ≪≫
263	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_mid D i j k)).app (M j) ≪≫
264	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i))
265	            (D.f j k) (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j) (g j k) ≪≫
266	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_tgt D i j k)).app (M k)
267	        = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_src D i j k)).app (M i) ≪≫
268	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
269	            (D.f i k) (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) (g i k)) :
270	    D.glued.Modules :=
271	  sorry
272	
273	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
