1	\chapter{Section graded ring infrastructure: tensor powers and graded sections}
2	\label{chap:Picard_SectionGradedRing}
3	
4	\providecommand{\PshMod}{\mathrm{PresheafOfModules}}
5	\providecommand{\ShMod}{\mathrm{SheafOfModules}}
6	\providecommand{\Modules}{\mathrm{Modules}}
7	
8	The Hilbert-polynomial route of \cref{chap:Picard_QuotScheme} represents the
9	degree-\(m\) graded component of a fibre by the global sections
10	\(\Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) of a twist of a coherent
11	sheaf by a tensor power of a line bundle, and it organises these components into
12	a graded ring \(R(X_s, L_s) = \bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m})\)
13	(\cref{def:sectionGradedRing}) and a graded module
14	\(M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s) = \bigoplus_{m \ge 0}
15	\Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\)
16	(\cref{def:sectionGradedModule}). Three pieces of that picture have no direct
17	Mathlib counterpart and must be built here, each in its own section:
18	
19	\begin{enumerate}
20	  \item \textbf{Tensor powers of a sheaf of modules}
21	    (\cref{sec:sgr_tensor_powers}). Mathlib equips the category
22	    \(\PshMod\) of \emph{presheaves} of modules over a presheaf of commutative
23	    rings with a symmetric monoidal structure
24	    (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}) and provides the
25	    sheafification functor \(\PshMod \to \ShMod\)
26	    (\cref{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}); but the category
27	    \(\ShMod\) of \emph{sheaves} of modules carries \emph{no} monoidal
28	    structure. The tensor product of sheaves of modules
29	    \(\mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G}\) must therefore be built
30	    as the sheafification of the pointwise tensor presheaf, and the iterated
31	    tensor power \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m}\) recursively from it.
32	  \item \textbf{Lax-monoidal global sections} (\cref{sec:sgr_lax_sections}).
33	    The global-sections functor \(\Gamma\) is only \emph{lax} monoidal: from the
34	    pointwise-strict monoidality of the presheaf tensor product and the
35	    sheafification unit one obtains a natural multiplication
36	    \(\Gamma(\mathcal{F}) \otimes_{\Gamma(\mathcal{O}_X)} \Gamma(\mathcal{G})
37	    \to \Gamma(\mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G})\), together with
38	    the associativity and unit coherence those products must satisfy.
39	  \item \textbf{Graded-ring / graded-module assembly}
40	    (\cref{sec:sgr_graded_assembly}). The section multiplications and the
41	    tensor-power comparison isomorphisms assemble, through Mathlib's
42	    external-direct-sum graded API (\cref{lem:directSum_gcommSemiring_mathlib},
43	    \cref{lem:directSum_gmodule_mathlib}), into the graded \emph{ring} structure
44	    on \(\bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m})\) and the graded
45	    \emph{module} structure on
46	    \(\bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\).
47	\end{enumerate}
48	
49	\section{Tensor powers of a sheaf of modules}
50	\label{sec:sgr_tensor_powers}
51	
52	We work over a fixed scheme \(X\) (in the application \(X = X_s\), a fibre over a
53	field \(\kappa(s)\)). Its category of sheaves of modules is
54	\(X.\Modules = \ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\), with global-sections notation
55	\(\Gamma(X, \mathcal{M}) = \mathcal{M}(X)\) for the value of the underlying
56	presheaf on the top open. The structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_X\) is a sheaf of
57	commutative rings, so its underlying presheaf factors through the category of
58	commutative rings; this is the hypothesis under which Mathlib's presheaf-level
59	monoidal structure applies.
60	
61	\begin{lemma}[Monoidal structure on presheaves of modules]
62	  \label{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}
63	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory}
64	  \mathlibok
65	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
66	  (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Monoidal}).}
67	  Let \(R : \mathcal{C}^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathrm{CommRingCat}\) be a presheaf of
68	  commutative rings. Then the category \(\PshMod(R)\) of presheaves of modules
69	  over \(R\) carries a symmetric monoidal structure whose tensor product is
70	  computed objectwise: for presheaves of modules \(\mathcal{M}_1, \mathcal{M}_2\)
71	  and an object \(U\),
72	  \[
73	    (\mathcal{M}_1 \otimes \mathcal{M}_2)(U)
74	      \;=\; \mathcal{M}_1(U) \otimes_{R(U)} \mathcal{M}_2(U),
75	  \]
76	  with restriction maps the tensor products of the restriction maps. The unit is
77	  the presheaf \(R\) viewed as a module over itself, and the associator,
78	  unitors, and braiding are induced objectwise from those of
79	  \(\mathrm{ModuleCat}\). In particular evaluation at a fixed object \(U\) is a
80	  \emph{strict} monoidal functor
81	  \(\PshMod(R) \to \mathrm{ModuleCat}(R(U))\).
82	\end{lemma}
83	
84	\begin{lemma}[Sheafification of presheaves of modules]
85	  \label{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
86	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}
87	  \mathlibok
88	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
89	  (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Sheafification}).}
90	  Let \(R\) be a sheaf of (commutative) rings on a site, with underlying
91	  presheaf \(R_0\). There is a sheafification functor
92	  \[
93	    (-)^{\#} \;:\; \PshMod(R_0) \longrightarrow \ShMod(R),
94	  \]
95	  left adjoint to the forgetful functor, together with the unit
96	  \(\eta_{\mathcal{M}_0} : \mathcal{M}_0 \to (\mathcal{M}_0)^{\#}\) of the
97	  adjunction. On underlying sheaves of abelian groups it agrees with the
98	  ordinary sheafification, and it sends a presheaf of \(R_0\)-modules to the
99	  associated sheaf of \(R\)-modules.
100	\end{lemma}
101	
102	\begin{lemma}[The structure sheaf as the unit module]
103	  \label{lem:moduleUnit_mathlib}
104	  \lean{SheafOfModules.unit}
105	  \mathlibok
106	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
107	  (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf}).}
108	  For a sheaf of rings \(R\) (in the application \(R = \mathcal{O}_X\)) the
109	  structure sheaf, viewed as a module over itself, is a distinguished object
110	  \(\mathbf{1} = \mathcal{O}_X \in \ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\). It is the value at
111	  the zeroth tensor power, \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes 0} = \mathcal{O}_X\), and its
112	  global sections \(\Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) form the degree-zero component of
113	  the section graded ring.
114	\end{lemma}
115	
116	\begin{definition}\leanok
117	[Presheaf-of-modules category in monoidal form]
118	  \label{def:monoidalPresheaf}
119	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.MonoidalPresheaf}
120	  \uses{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}
121	  For a scheme \(X\), write \(\PshMod_{\otimes}(X)\) for the category of presheaves
122	  of modules over the underlying presheaf of (plain) rings of \(\mathcal{O}_X\),
123	  obtained by composing the structure sheaf with the forgetful functor
124	  \(\mathrm{CommRingCat} \to \mathrm{RingCat}\). This is definitionally
125	  \(X.\PshMod\), but presented in the exact form for which Mathlib equips it with
126	  the symmetric monoidal structure of \cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib};
127	  it is the carrier on which the objectwise tensor product of
128	  \cref{def:sheafTensorObj} is taken.
129	\end{definition}
130	
131	\begin{definition}\leanok
132	[Scheme-level module sheafification functor]
133	  \label{def:schemeModuleSheafification}
134	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheafification}
135	  \uses{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
136	  For a scheme \(X\), the \emph{module sheafification functor}
137	  \((-)^{\#} : X.\PshMod \to X.\Modules\) is Mathlib's sheafification functor
138	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}) specialised to the identity
139	  morphism of the underlying presheaf of rings of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) (a locally
140	  bijective map). Applying it to the objectwise tensor presheaf is what defines the
141	  sheaf tensor product \cref{def:sheafTensorObj}.
142	\end{definition}
143	
144	\begin{definition}\leanok
145	[Unit module of a scheme]
146	  \label{def:unitModule}
147	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.unitModule}
148	  \uses{lem:moduleUnit_mathlib}
149	  For a scheme \(X\), the \emph{unit module} \(\mathbf{1}_X \in X.\Modules\) is the
150	  structure sheaf viewed as a module over itself, i.e.\ Mathlib's
151	  \(\mathrm{SheafOfModules.unit}\) (\cref{lem:moduleUnit_mathlib}) for
152	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\). It is the value of the zeroth tensor power,
153	  \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes 0} = \mathbf{1}_X\) (\cref{def:sheafTensorPow}).
154	\end{definition}
155	
156	\begin{definition}\leanok
157	[Tensor product of sheaves of modules]
158	  \label{def:sheafTensorObj}
159	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj}
160	  \uses{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
161	    lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
162	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Sheaves of Modules", \S "Tensor product" (Tag
163	  % 01CA), prose definition (read from references/stacks-modules.tex,
164	  % L2282-L2297).
165	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
166	  % "Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space and let
167	  %  $\mathcal{F}$ and $\mathcal{G}$ be $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules.
168	  %  We define first the {\it tensor product presheaf}
169	  %  $$
170	  %  \mathcal{F} \otimes_{p, \mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G}
171	  %  $$
172	  %  as the rule which assigns to $U \subset X$ open the
173	  %  $\mathcal{O}_X(U)$-module
174	  %  $\mathcal{F}(U) \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)} \mathcal{G}(U)$.
175	  %  Having defined this we define the {\it tensor product sheaf}
176	  %  as the sheafification of the above:
177	  %  $$
178	  %  \mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G}
179	  %  =
180	  %  (\mathcal{F} \otimes_{p, \mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G})^\#
181	  %  $$"
182	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Sheaves of Modules", Tag 01CA (tensor
183	  product).}
184	  Let \(\mathcal{F}, \mathcal{G}\) be sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. Their
185	  \emph{tensor product} is the sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
186	  \[
187	    \mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G}
188	      \;=\; \bigl(\mathcal{F} \otimes_{p,\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G}\bigr)^{\#},
189	  \]
190	  the sheafification (\cref{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}) of the
191	  tensor product presheaf \(\mathcal{F} \otimes_{p,\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G}\),
192	  whose value on an open \(U\) is the \(\mathcal{O}_X(U)\)-module
193	  \(\mathcal{F}(U) \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)} \mathcal{G}(U)\). Concretely, the
194	  tensor product presheaf is the Mathlib presheaf-level monoidal product
195	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}) of the underlying presheaves of
196	  modules of \(\mathcal{F}\) and \(\mathcal{G}\), and
197	  \(\mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G}\) is its sheafification. This
198	  construction is symmetric and associative because the presheaf tensor product
199	  is, and sheafification preserves the coherence isomorphisms.
200	\end{definition}
201	
202	\begin{definition}\leanok
203	[Tensor power of a line bundle]
204	  \label{def:sheafTensorPow}
205	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorPow}
206	  \uses{def:sheafTensorObj, def:unitModule, lem:moduleUnit_mathlib}
207	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Sheaves of Modules", \S "Invertible modules",
208	  % `definition-powers' (Tag 01CU) (read from references/stacks-modules.tex,
209	  % L4220-L4228).
210	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
211	  % "Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space. Given an invertible sheaf
212	  %  $\mathcal{L}$ on $X$ and $n \in \mathbf{Z}$ we define the
213	  %  $n$th {\it tensor power} $\mathcal{L}^{\otimes n}$ of $\mathcal{L}$
214	  %  as the image of $\mathcal{O}_X$ under applying the equivalence
215	  %  $\mathcal{F} \mapsto\mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{L}$
216	  %  exactly $n$ times."
217	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Sheaves of Modules", Tag 01CU (tensor
218	  powers).}
219	  Let \(\mathcal{L}\) be a sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules (in the application
220	  an invertible sheaf / line bundle \(L_s\)). For \(m \in \mathbb{N}\) the
221	  \emph{\(m\)th tensor power} \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m}\) is defined by recursion
222	  on \(m\):
223	  \[
224	    \mathcal{L}^{\otimes 0} = \mathcal{O}_X
225	      \quad(\text{the unit module, \cref{lem:moduleUnit_mathlib}}),
226	    \qquad
227	    \mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m+1)}
228	      = \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{L}
229	        \quad(\cref{def:sheafTensorObj}).
230	  \]
231	  We restrict to non-negative powers, which is all the section graded ring uses;
232	  the Stacks definition extends \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes n}\) to all
233	  \(n \in \mathbf{Z}\) using invertibility, but negative powers are not needed
234	  here. For \(\mathcal{L}\) invertible each \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m}\) is again
235	  invertible.
236	\end{definition}
237	
238	\begin{lemma}\leanok
239	[Zeroth tensor power is the unit]
240	  \label{lem:tensorPow_zero}
241	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorPow_zero}
242	  \uses{def:sheafTensorPow, def:unitModule}
243	  For a sheaf of modules \(\mathcal{L}\) on \(X\) one has
244	  \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes 0} = \mathbf{1}_X\), the unit module
245	  (\cref{def:unitModule}); this is the base clause of the recursive definition
246	  \cref{def:sheafTensorPow}, holding by definition.
247	\end{lemma}
248	
249	\begin{lemma}\leanok
250	[Tensor-power recursion step]
251	  \label{lem:tensorPow_succ}
252	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorPow_succ}
253	  \uses{def:sheafTensorPow, def:sheafTensorObj}
254	  For a sheaf of modules \(\mathcal{L}\) on \(X\) and \(m \in \mathbb{N}\) one has
255	  \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m+1)} = \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X}
256	  \mathcal{L}\) (\cref{def:sheafTensorObj}); this is the successor clause of
257	  \cref{def:sheafTensorPow}, holding by definition.
258	\end{lemma}

260	\begin{definition}\leanok
261	[Twist of a coherent sheaf by a tensor power]
262	  \label{def:sheafModuleTwist}
263	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.moduleTensorPow}
264	  \uses{def:sheafTensorObj, def:sheafTensorPow}
265	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules (in the application
266	  a coherent sheaf \(\mathcal{F}_s\)) and \(\mathcal{L}\) a line bundle. For
267	  \(m \in \mathbb{N}\) the \emph{\(m\)-twist} of \(\mathcal{F}\) by
268	  \(\mathcal{L}\) is
269	  \[
270	    \mathcal{F}(m) \;:=\;
271	      \mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m}
272	      \qquad(\cref{def:sheafTensorObj}, \cref{def:sheafTensorPow}),
273	  \]
274	  the degree-\(m\) carrier of the section graded module. By construction
275	  \(\mathcal{F}(0) = \mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{O}_X \cong
276	  \mathcal{F}\).
277	\end{definition}

279	\begin{lemma}\leanok
280	[Zeroth twist]
281	  \label{lem:moduleTensorPow_zero}
282	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.moduleTensorPow_zero}
283	  \uses{def:sheafModuleTwist, def:sheafTensorObj, def:unitModule,
284	    def:tensorObjRightUnitor}
285	  For sheaves of modules \(\mathcal{F}, \mathcal{L}\) on \(X\) one has
286	  \(\mathcal{F}(0) = \mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathbf{1}_X\)
287	  (\cref{def:sheafTensorObj}, \cref{def:unitModule}); this is the base clause of
288	  \cref{def:sheafModuleTwist}, holding by definition, and is canonically
289	  isomorphic to \(\mathcal{F}\) via the right unitor
290	  (\cref{def:tensorObjRightUnitor}).
291	\end{lemma}

293	The next four isomorphisms are the parts of the (would-be) symmetric monoidal
294	structure on \(X.\Modules\) that descend through sheafification from

296	sheafification functor (\cref{def:schemeModuleSheafification}) and, for the
297	unitors, the reflective counit isomorphism --- no strong monoidality of
298	sheafification is required. They are the structural inputs of the tensor-power

323	  \cref{def:unitModule}), obtained by sheafifying the presheaf left unitor of
324	  \cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib} and composing with the counit
325	  isomorphism \cref{def:sheafificationCounitIso}. It is the base case
326	  (\(m = 0\)) of \cref{lem:sheafTensorPow_add}.
327	\end{definition}
328	
329	\begin{definition}\leanok
330	[Right unitor of the sheaf tensor product]
331	  \label{def:tensorObjRightUnitor}
332	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjRightUnitor}
333	  \uses{def:sheafTensorObj, def:unitModule, def:sheafificationCounitIso,
334	    lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}
335	  For a sheaf of modules \(\mathcal{G}\) on \(X\) there is a canonical right-unitor
336	  isomorphism \(\mathcal{G} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathbf{1}_X
337	  \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ } \mathcal{G}\) (\cref{def:sheafTensorObj},
338	  \cref{def:unitModule}), the sheafification of the presheaf right unitor
339	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}) composed with the counit
340	  isomorphism \cref{def:sheafificationCounitIso}.
341	\end{definition}
342	
343	\begin{definition}\leanok
344	[Braiding of the sheaf tensor product]
345	  \label{def:tensorBraiding}
346	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorBraiding}
347	  \uses{def:sheafTensorObj, def:schemeModuleSheafification,
348	    lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}
349	  For sheaves of modules \(\mathcal{F}, \mathcal{G}\) on \(X\) there is a canonical
350	  braiding isomorphism \(\mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G}
351	  \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ } \mathcal{G} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{F}\)
352	  (\cref{def:sheafTensorObj}), obtained by sheafifying
353	  (\cref{def:schemeModuleSheafification}) the symmetric braiding of the presheaf
354	  monoidal structure (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}). It is the
355	  symmetry used in the inductive step of \cref{lem:sheafTensorPow_add}.
356	\end{definition}
357	
358	The strong-monoidality comparison \cref{lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit}
359	below is assembled from three reduction lemmas --- a localization criterion, the
360	local-isomorphism property of the unit, and their corollary --- together with the
361	coequalizer presentation of the relative module-presheaf tensor product, the single
362	Mathlib-absent brick on which the comparison rests.
363	
364	\begin{lemma}[Localization criterion for module sheafification]
365	  \label{lem:isIso_sheafification_map_iff}
366	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_sheafification_map_iff}
367	  \uses{def:schemeModuleSheafification, lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
368	  For a morphism \(f : \mathcal{P} \to \mathcal{Q}\) of presheaves of
369	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, the sheafification \((f)^{\#}\)
370	  (\cref{def:schemeModuleSheafification}) is an isomorphism of sheaves of modules if
371	  and only if the underlying morphism of abelian-group presheaves lies in the
372	  weak-equivalence class \(J.W\) of the opens topology \(J\) on \(X\) (i.e.\ is a
373	  local isomorphism of abelian-group presheaves).
374	\end{lemma}
375	
376	\begin{proof}
377	  \uses{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
378	  Module sheafification (\cref{def:schemeModuleSheafification}) is the localization
379	  functor at the class \(W\) of morphisms of presheaves of modules whose underlying
380	  abelian-presheaf morphism lies in \(J.W\), i.e.\ the inverse image of \(J.W\) along
381	  the forgetful functor. The localization criterion for a reflective adjunction
382	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}) then gives the stated
383	  equivalence.
384	\end{proof}
385	
386	\begin{lemma}[The sheafification unit is an abelian local isomorphism]
387	  \label{lem:localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit}
388	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit}
389	  \uses{def:schemeModuleSheafification, lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
390	  For a presheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\mathcal{P}\) the underlying
391	  abelian-group presheaf morphism of the sheafification unit
392	  \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} : \mathcal{P} \to (\mathcal{P})^{\#}\)
393	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}) is the abelian sheafification
394	  unit \(\mathcal{P} \to a(\mathcal{P})\); it therefore lies in the weak-equivalence
395	  class \(J.W\) of the opens topology.
396	\end{lemma}
397	
398	\begin{proof}
399	  \uses{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
400	  The forgetful functor to abelian-group presheaves carries the module
401	  sheafification unit to the unit of the abelian associated-sheaf adjunction, which
402	  is a local isomorphism, hence a member of \(J.W\).
403	\end{proof}
404	
405	\begin{lemma}[Sheafification inverts the localization unit]
406	  \label{lem:isIso_sheafification_map_unit}
407	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_sheafification_map_unit}
408	  \uses{lem:isIso_sheafification_map_iff, lem:localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit}
409	  For a presheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\mathcal{P}\) the sheafification
410	  \((\eta_{\mathcal{P}})^{\#}\) of the localization unit is an isomorphism of sheaves
411	  of modules. This is the un-whiskered (\(m = 0\)) special case of
412	  \cref{lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit}.
413	\end{lemma}
414	
415	\begin{proof}
416	  \uses{lem:isIso_sheafification_map_iff, lem:localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit}
417	  Feed the abelian local-isomorphism statement
418	  \cref{lem:localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit} through the localization criterion
419	  \cref{lem:isIso_sheafification_map_iff}.
420	\end{proof}
421	
422	\begin{lemma}[Relative tensor of module presheaves as a coequalizer]
423	  \label{lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer}
424	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.relativeTensorCoequalizerIso}
425	  \uses{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}
426	  Let \(\mathcal{P}, \mathcal{Q}\) be presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. The
427	  underlying abelian-group presheaf of their relative tensor product
428	  \(\mathcal{P} \otimes_{p,\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{Q}\)
429	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}) is naturally the coequalizer of the
430	  parallel pair
431	  \[
432	    \mathcal{P} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{O}_X \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}
433	    \;\rightrightarrows\;
434	    \mathcal{P} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}
435	  \]
436	  of abelian-group presheaves, where the first map acts the ring factor on the left,
437	  \(p \otimes r \otimes q \mapsto (r \cdot p) \otimes q\), and the second on the
438	  right, \(p \otimes r \otimes q \mapsto p \otimes (r \cdot q)\). The identification
439	  is objectwise and natural in \(U\).
440	\end{lemma}
441	
442	\begin{proof}
443	  \uses{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}
444	  Over each open \(U\) the module
445	  \(\mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)} \mathcal{Q}(U)\) is, by the very
446	  definition of the relative tensor product, the quotient of the \(\mathbb{Z}\)-tensor
447	  \(\mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}(U)\) by the subgroup generated by
448	  the elements \((r \cdot p) \otimes q - p \otimes (r \cdot q)\); equivalently it is
449	  the coequalizer of the two \(\mathbb{Z}\)-linear action maps displayed above. Since
450	  colimits of abelian-group presheaves are computed objectwise, these objectwise
451	  coequalizers assemble into a coequalizer in
452	  \(\mathcal{C}^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathrm{AddCommGrp}\); naturality in \(U\) is the
453	  compatibility of the action maps with the restriction maps of
454	  \(\mathcal{P}, \mathcal{O}_X, \mathcal{Q}\).
455	\end{proof}
456	
457	\begin{lemma}[Sheafification inverts the whiskered localization unit]
458	  \label{lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit}
459	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit}
460	  \uses{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
461	    lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib,
462	    lem:isIso_sheafification_map_iff, lem:localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit,
463	    lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer}
464	  Let \(\mathcal{P}, \mathcal{Q}\) be presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules,
465	  let \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} : \mathcal{P} \to (\mathcal{P})^{\#}\) be the unit of
466	  the sheafification adjunction
467	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}), viewed as a morphism of
468	  presheaves of modules, and write
469	  \[
470	    \eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd \mathcal{Q} :
471	    \mathcal{P} \otimes_p \mathcal{Q}
472	    \longrightarrow (\mathcal{P})^{\#} \otimes_p \mathcal{Q}
473	  \]
474	  for its right whiskering by \(\mathcal{Q}\) in the presheaf monoidal structure
475	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}). Then the image of
476	  \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd \mathcal{Q}\) under the sheafification functor,
477	  \[
478	    (\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd \mathcal{Q})^{\#} :
479	    (\mathcal{P} \otimes_p \mathcal{Q})^{\#}
480	    \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }
481	    \bigl((\mathcal{P})^{\#} \otimes_p \mathcal{Q}\bigr)^{\#},
482	  \]
483	  is an isomorphism of sheaves of modules. This is the strong-monoidality
484	  comparison of the module sheafification functor on a whiskered unit.
485	\end{lemma}
486	
487	\begin{proof}
488	  \uses{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
489	    lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib,
490	    lem:isIso_sheafification_map_iff, lem:localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit,
491	    lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer}
492	  Module sheafification is a localization functor: it inverts exactly the class
493	  \(W\) of morphisms of presheaves of modules whose underlying morphism of
494	  abelian-group presheaves is a local isomorphism --- that is, lies in the class
495	  \(J.W\) of weak equivalences attached to the Grothendieck topology \(J\) on
496	  \(X\) (the inverse image of \(J.W\) along the forgetful functor to
497	  abelian-group presheaves). By the localization criterion for a reflective
498	  adjunction, \((f)^{\#}\) is an isomorphism if and only if \(f \in W\), i.e.\
499	  if and only if the underlying abelian-presheaf morphism of \(f\) lies in
500	  \(J.W\). It therefore suffices to prove that the underlying abelian morphism
501	  of \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd \mathcal{Q}\) lies in \(J.W\).
502	
503	  \textbf{The relative tensor as a coequalizer.} The underlying abelian-group
504	  presheaf of the relative tensor product \(\mathcal{P} \otimes_p \mathcal{Q}\)
505	  is the objectwise relative tensor
506	  \(U \mapsto \mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)} \mathcal{Q}(U)\). For each
507	  \(U\) this module is the coequalizer of the two \(\mathcal{O}_X(U)\)-action
508	  maps
509	  \[
510	    \mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{O}_X(U)
511	      \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}(U)
512	    \;\rightrightarrows\;
513	    \mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}(U),
514	  \]
515	  one acting on the left and one on the right factor. Colimits of abelian-group
516	  presheaves are computed objectwise, so \(\mathcal{P} \otimes_p \mathcal{Q}\),
517	  as an abelian-group presheaf, is the coequalizer of the corresponding parallel
518	  pair
519	  \[
520	    \mathcal{P} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{O}_X
521	      \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}
522	    \;\rightrightarrows\;
523	    \mathcal{P} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}
524	  \]
525	  of \(\mathbb{Z}\)-tensor presheaves. Under this presentation the underlying
526	  abelian morphism of \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd \mathcal{Q}\) is the map of
527	  coequalizers induced by whiskering the two rows with
528	  \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} (-)\).
529	
530	  \textbf{Abelian sheafification preserves the coequalizer.} The abelian
531	  sheafification functor \(a\) (the associated-sheaf functor for \(J\) on
532	  abelian-group presheaves) is a left adjoint, hence preserves coequalizers; it
533	  therefore carries the parallel pair and its induced map to the analogous data
534	  of \(a\)-images. Now the localization unit \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}}\) lies in
535	  \(J.W\), and the class \(J.W\) is \emph{monoidal} for the
536	  \(\mathbb{Z}\)-tensor: whiskering a local isomorphism by
537	  \(\otimes_{\mathbb{Z}}\) again yields a local isomorphism. (This is the
538	  abelian monoidality of \(J.W\), itself proved through the fact that the
539	  internal hom into a sheaf is again a sheaf.) Consequently the two whiskered
540	  comparison maps
541	  \(a\bigl(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathrm{id}\bigr)\) and
542	  \(a\bigl(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathrm{id}
543	  \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathrm{id}\bigr)\) relating the two rows are
544	  isomorphisms.
545	
546	  An isomorphism between two parallel-pair diagrams induces an isomorphism of
547	  their coequalizers; applied to the rows above, this shows that \(a\) sends the
548	  underlying abelian morphism of \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd \mathcal{Q}\) to an
549	  isomorphism. Hence that morphism lies in \(J.W\), so
550	  \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd \mathcal{Q} \in W\), and
551	  \((\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd \mathcal{Q})^{\#}\) is an isomorphism.
552	\end{proof}
553	
554	\begin{corollary}[Sheaf-level associator on tensor products]
555	  \label{cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc}
556	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjAssoc}
557	  \uses{def:sheafTensorObj, lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit,
558	    lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
559	    lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
560	  For sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\mathcal{A}, \mathcal{B},
561	  \mathcal{C}\) there is a canonical associativity isomorphism
562	  \[
563	    \alpha_{\mathcal{A},\mathcal{B},\mathcal{C}} :
564	    (\mathcal{A} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{B})
565	      \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{C}
566	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
567	    \mathcal{A} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X}
568	      (\mathcal{B} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{C})
569	  \]
570	  of sheaves of modules (\cref{def:sheafTensorObj}), natural in all three
571	  arguments.
572	\end{corollary}
573	
574	\begin{proof}
575	  \uses{def:sheafTensorObj, lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit,
576	    lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
577	    lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
578	  Write \(a, b, c\) for the underlying presheaves of modules of
579	  \(\mathcal{A}, \mathcal{B}, \mathcal{C}\) and \((-)^{\#}\) for sheafification
580	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}). By
581	  \cref{def:sheafTensorObj} the two iterated products unfold to
582	  \[
583	    (\mathcal{A} \otimes \mathcal{B}) \otimes \mathcal{C}
584	      = \bigl((a \otimes_p b)^{\#} \otimes_p c\bigr)^{\#},
585	    \qquad
586	    \mathcal{A} \otimes (\mathcal{B} \otimes \mathcal{C})
587	      = \bigl(a \otimes_p (b \otimes_p c)^{\#}\bigr)^{\#},
588	  \]
589	  each carrying an \emph{inner} sheafification that obstructs a direct appeal to
590	  the presheaf associator. The whiskered units remove these inner
591	  sheafifications. Applying \cref{lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit}
592	  with \(\mathcal{P} = a \otimes_p b\) and \(\mathcal{Q} = c\), the morphism
593	  \[
594	    (\eta_{a \otimes_p b} \rhd c)^{\#} :
595	    \bigl((a \otimes_p b) \otimes_p c\bigr)^{\#}
596	    \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }
597	    \bigl((a \otimes_p b)^{\#} \otimes_p c\bigr)^{\#}
598	    = (\mathcal{A} \otimes \mathcal{B}) \otimes \mathcal{C}
599	  \]
600	  is an isomorphism; and, reading the same lemma through the braiding of the
601	  presheaf monoidal structure (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}) so as
602	  to whisker the unit \(\eta_{b \otimes_p c}\) on the left of \(a\), the
603	  morphism
604	  \[
605	    (a \lhd \eta_{b \otimes_p c})^{\#} :
606	    \bigl(a \otimes_p (b \otimes_p c)\bigr)^{\#}
607	    \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }
608	    \bigl(a \otimes_p (b \otimes_p c)^{\#}\bigr)^{\#}
609	    = \mathcal{A} \otimes (\mathcal{B} \otimes \mathcal{C})
610	  \]
611	  is an isomorphism. Between the two un-sheafified middle terms sits the
612	  sheafification of the presheaf associator
613	  \[
614	    \alpha^p_{a,b,c} :
615	    (a \otimes_p b) \otimes_p c
616	    \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }
617	    a \otimes_p (b \otimes_p c)
618	  \]
619	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}), an isomorphism because
620	  sheafification is a functor and \(\alpha^p\) is one. The associator is the
621	  composite
622	  \[
623	    \alpha_{\mathcal{A},\mathcal{B},\mathcal{C}}
624	    = (a \lhd \eta_{b \otimes_p c})^{\#}
625	      \circ (\alpha^p_{a,b,c})^{\#}
626	      \circ \bigl((\eta_{a \otimes_p b} \rhd c)^{\#}\bigr)^{-1},
627	  \]
628	  an isomorphism as a composite of isomorphisms. Naturality in
629	  \(\mathcal{A}, \mathcal{B}, \mathcal{C}\) follows from naturality of the unit,
630	  of the whiskerings, and of the presheaf associator.
631	\end{proof}
632	
633	\begin{lemma}[Tensor-power comparison isomorphism]
634	  \label{lem:sheafTensorPow_add}
635	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorPowAdd}
636	  \uses{def:sheafTensorPow, def:sheafTensorObj}
637	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Sheaves of Modules", \S "Invertible modules"
638	  % (Tag 01CU, following `definition-powers') (read from
639	  % references/stacks-modules.tex, L4249-L4254).
640	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
641	  % "With this definition we have canonical isomorphisms
642	  %  $\mathcal{L}^{\otimes n} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X}
643	  %  \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \to
644	  %  \mathcal{L}^{\otimes n + m}$, and these isomorphisms
645	  %  satisfy a commutativity and an associativity constraint
646	  %  (formulation omitted)."
647	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Sheaves of Modules", Tag 01CU (canonical
648	  power isomorphisms).}
649	  For a line bundle \(\mathcal{L}\) and \(m, m' \in \mathbb{N}\) there is a
650	  canonical isomorphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
651	  \[
652	    \mu_{m,m'} :
653	    \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}
654	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; \mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m+m')},
655	  \]
656	  natural in \(\mathcal{L}\), and these isomorphisms satisfy a commutativity
657	  constraint (\(\mu_{m,m'}\) agrees with \(\mu_{m',m}\) after the braiding) and
658	  an associativity constraint (the two ways of combining
659	  \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'} \otimes
660	  \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m''}\) into \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m+m'+m'')}\) agree).
661	\end{lemma}
662	
663	\begin{proof}
664	  \uses{def:sheafTensorPow, def:sheafTensorObj, cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc,
665	    def:tensorObjUnitIso, def:tensorBraiding,
666	    lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit,
667	    lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
668	    lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
669	  We do \emph{not} build a full monoidal structure on the whole category
670	  \(\ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\) --- none is constructed here --- but only assemble
671	  the comparison \(\mu_{m,m'}\) from those structural isomorphisms of
672	  \cref{def:sheafTensorObj} that the induction actually uses: the associator,
673	  the braiding, and the left unitor.
674	
675	  \textbf{The structural maps.} The braiding and the unitors descend to
676	  \(\ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\) directly: each is the sheafification of the
677	  corresponding isomorphism of the presheaf symmetric monoidal structure
678	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}), and sheafification, being a
679	  functor, carries isomorphisms to isomorphisms. The \emph{associator} is not
680	  the sheafification of a single presheaf isomorphism, because the iterated
681	  sheaf tensor products
682	  \((\mathcal{A} \otimes \mathcal{B}) \otimes \mathcal{C}\) and
683	  \(\mathcal{A} \otimes (\mathcal{B} \otimes \mathcal{C})\) each carry an inner
684	  sheafification (\cref{def:sheafTensorObj}); it is supplied instead by
685	  \cref{cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc}, whose construction clears those inner
686	  sheafifications using the strong-monoidality comparison
687	  \cref{lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit}. These are the only
688	  isomorphisms of sheaves of modules the construction invokes; right-exactness
689	  of the tensor product is never used.
690	
691	  \textbf{Construction of \(\mu_{m,m'}\).} Fix \(m'\) and induct on \(m\). For
692	  \(m = 0\),
693	  \[
694	    \mathcal{L}^{\otimes 0} \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}
695	      = \mathcal{O}_X \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}
696	      \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ } \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}
697	  \]
698	  is the left unitor of \cref{def:sheafTensorObj}. For the inductive step the
699	  recursive definition
700	  \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m+1)} = \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes \mathcal{L}\)
701	  (\cref{def:sheafTensorPow}) gives
702	  \[
703	    \mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m+1)} \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}
704	    = (\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes \mathcal{L}) \otimes
705	      \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}
706	    \xrightarrow{\ \alpha\ } \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes
707	      (\mathcal{L} \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'})
708	    \xrightarrow{\ \mathrm{id} \otimes \beta\ } \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes
709	      (\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'} \otimes \mathcal{L}),
710	  \]
711	  using the associator \(\alpha\) (\cref{cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc}) and then the
712	  braiding \(\beta\) of \cref{def:sheafTensorObj} whiskered into the left
713	  factor. The inverse associator \(\alpha^{-1}\)
714	  (\cref{cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc}) and the inductive comparison \(\mu_{m,m'}\)
715	  whiskered by \(\mathcal{L}\) on the right then carry this to
716	  \[
717	    (\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}) \otimes
718	      \mathcal{L}
719	    \xrightarrow{\ \mu_{m,m'} \rhd \mathcal{L}\ }
720	    \mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m + m')} \otimes \mathcal{L}
721	    = \mathcal{L}^{\otimes ((m + m') + 1)},
722	  \]
723	  the final equality being the recursive definition. The reindexing
724	  \((m + m') + 1 = (m + 1) + m'\) identifies the target with
725	  \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes ((m+1) + m')}\), and the composite is
726	  \(\mu_{m+1,m'}\), an isomorphism as a composite of isomorphisms. Naturality in
727	  \(\mathcal{L}\) holds because each constituent comparison is natural.
728	
729	  \textbf{Commutativity and associativity constraints.} The braiding and
730	  unitors are the sheafifications of the symmetric-monoidal coherence data of
731	  \(\PshMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\) (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}), and
732	  the associator is intertwined with the presheaf associator by the comparison
733	  isomorphisms of \cref{lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit} through the
734	  construction of \cref{cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc}. Both the commutativity square
735	  (\(\mu_{m,m'}\) versus \(\mu_{m',m}\) postcomposed with the braiding) and the
736	  associativity pentagon (the two bracketings of
737	  \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'} \otimes
738	  \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m''} \to \mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m+m'+m'')}\)) are therefore
739	  images, under the sheafification functor together with these comparison
740	  isomorphisms, of the corresponding coherence diagrams of the presheaf
741	  symmetric monoidal structure, which commute by Mac\,Lane coherence. Since a
742	  functor preserves commuting diagrams and the comparison isomorphisms are
743	  natural, the constraints hold for the family \(\{\mu_{m,m'}\}\). We emphasise
744	  that this argument uses only the single strong-monoidality comparison
745	  \cref{lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit} and the resulting associator
746	  \cref{cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc}, not a full monoidal structure on
747	  \(\ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\).
748	\end{proof}
749	
750	\section{Lax-monoidal global sections}
751	\label{sec:sgr_lax_sections}
752	
753	The graded multiplication is supplied by the lax-monoidal structure of the
754	global-sections functor \(\Gamma(X, -) : \ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X) \to
755	\mathrm{ModuleCat}(\Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X))\). It is only \emph{lax} because
756	global sections do not commute with sheafification: a global section of a
757	sheafified tensor product is not in general a finite sum of elementary tensors of
758	global sections.
759	
760	\begin{definition}\leanok
761	[Section multiplication]
762	  \label{def:sectionMul}
763	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sectionsMul}
764	  \uses{def:sheafTensorObj, lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
765	    lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
766	  Let \(\mathcal{F}, \mathcal{G}\) be sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. The
767	  \emph{section multiplication} is the \(\Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X)\)-bilinear map
768	  \[
769	    \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_{\Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X)}
770	      \Gamma(X, \mathcal{G})
771	    \;\longrightarrow\;
772	    \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G})
773	  \]
774	  defined as follows. A pair of global sections \((\sigma, \tau)\) determines,
775	  by the objectwise formula of \cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib} at the
776	  top open, the elementary tensor \(\sigma \otimes \tau \in
777	  (\mathcal{F} \otimes_{p,\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G})(X)\), a global section of
778	  the tensor product presheaf. Composing with the global-sections component of
779	  the sheafification unit
780	  \(\eta : \mathcal{F} \otimes_{p,\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G} \to
781	  (\mathcal{F} \otimes_{p,\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G})^{\#} =
782	  \mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G}\)
783	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib},
784	  \cref{def:sheafTensorObj}) yields a global section
785	  \(\sigma \cdot \tau \in
786	  \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G})\). Bilinearity over
787	  \(\Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X)\) is inherited from bilinearity of the elementary
788	  tensor and \(\Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X)\)-linearity of \(\eta\).
789	\end{definition}
790	
791	\begin{lemma}[Coherence of the section multiplication]
792	  \label{lem:sectionMul_coherent}
793	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sectionsMul_assoc_unit}
794	  \uses{def:sectionMul, lem:moduleUnit_mathlib, lem:sheafTensorPow_add}
795	  The section multiplications (\cref{def:sectionMul}) are associative and unital
796	  in the following sense. For sheaves \(\mathcal{F}, \mathcal{G}, \mathcal{H}\)
797	  the two composites
798	  \[
799	    \Gamma(\mathcal{F}) \otimes \Gamma(\mathcal{G}) \otimes \Gamma(\mathcal{H})
800	    \longrightarrow
801	    \Gamma\bigl((\mathcal{F} \otimes \mathcal{G}) \otimes \mathcal{H}\bigr)
802	    \cong
803	    \Gamma\bigl(\mathcal{F} \otimes (\mathcal{G} \otimes \mathcal{H})\bigr)
804	  \]
805	  agree after transport along the associator of \cref{def:sheafTensorObj}; and
806	  the unit element \(1 \in \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\)
807	  (\cref{lem:moduleUnit_mathlib}) acts as a two-sided identity, i.e.\ the section
808	  multiplication by \(1\) is, after the unitor isomorphism
809	  \(\mathcal{O}_X \otimes \mathcal{F} \cong \mathcal{F}\), the identity on
810	  \(\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})\).
811	\end{lemma}
812	
813	\begin{proof}
814	  \uses{def:sectionMul, lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
815	    lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
816	  Both statements reduce to the corresponding identities at the level of the
817	  tensor product presheaf, where by \cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}
818	  evaluation at the top open is strictly monoidal, so the associator and unitors
819	  act on elementary tensors by the usual formulas
820	  \((\sigma \otimes \tau) \otimes \upsilon \mapsto
821	  \sigma \otimes (\tau \otimes \upsilon)\) and
822	  \(1 \otimes \sigma \mapsto \sigma\). The sheafification unit \(\eta\) is a
823	  natural transformation, so it commutes with the associator and unitor
824	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}); applying \(\Gamma(X,-)\)
825	  to the naturality squares transports these presheaf-level identities to the
826	  section multiplications.
827	\end{proof}
828	
829	\section{Graded-ring and graded-module assembly}

import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Basic
import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.Grp.Basic
import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.Equalizers

open CategoryTheory Limits TensorProduct

variable (S : Type) [CommRing S] (M N : Type)
  [AddCommGroup M] [Module S M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module S N]

-- domain D = M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N), codomain B = M ⊗[ℤ] N
-- actL: m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ (s•m) ⊗ n
noncomputable def actL : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
  TensorProduct.lift
    (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun m sn => TensorProduct.lift
        (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun s n => (s • m) ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n)
          (by intro s1 s2 n; simp [add_smul, add_tmul])
          (by intro c s n; simp)
          (by intro s n1 n2; simp [tmul_add])
          (by intro c s n; simp)) sn)
      (by intro m1 m2 sn; induction sn with
          | zero => simp | tmul s n => simp [add_smul, add_tmul] | add x y hx hy => simp_all)
      (by intro c m sn; induction sn with
          | zero => simp | tmul s n => simp [smul_smul, mul_comm] | add x y hx hy => simp_all [smul_add])
      (by intro m sn1 sn2; simp)
      (by intro c m sn; simp))

#check @actL

import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Basic
import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.Grp.Basic

open TensorProduct

variable (S : Type) [CommRing S] (M N : Type)
  [AddCommGroup M] [Module S M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module S N]

-- action map S ⊗[ℤ] N → N
noncomputable def actN : (S ⊗[ℤ] N) →ₗ[ℤ] N :=
  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun s n => s • n)
    (fun s1 s2 n => add_smul s1 s2 n)
    (fun c s n => smul_assoc c s n)
    (fun s n1 n2 => smul_add s n1 n2)
    (fun s c n => smul_comm s c n))

-- action map M ⊗[ℤ] S → M
noncomputable def actM : (M ⊗[ℤ] S) →ₗ[ℤ] M :=
  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun m s => s • m)
    (fun m1 m2 s => smul_add s m1 m2)
    (fun c m s => smul_comm s c m)
    (fun m s1 s2 => add_smul s1 s2 m)
    (fun m c s => smul_assoc c s m))

-- actR = lTensor M actN : M ⊗ (S ⊗ N) → M ⊗ N
noncomputable def actRmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
  LinearMap.lTensor M (actN S N)

-- actL = rTensor N actM ∘ assoc⁻¹ : M ⊗ (S ⊗ N) → (M ⊗ S) ⊗ N → M ⊗ N
noncomputable def actLmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
  (LinearMap.rTensor N (actM S M)).comp (TensorProduct.assoc ℤ M S N).symm.toLinearMap

example (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) :
    actRmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = m ⊗ₜ (s • n) := by
  simp [actRmap, actN]

example (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) :
    actLmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = (s • m) ⊗ₜ n := by
  simp [actLmap, actM]

import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Map
import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.Grp.Basic

open TensorProduct

variable (S : Type) [CommRing S] (M N : Type)
  [AddCommGroup M] [Module S M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module S N]

noncomputable def actN : (S ⊗[ℤ] N) →ₗ[ℤ] N :=
  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun s n => s • n)
    (fun s1 s2 n => add_smul s1 s2 n) (fun c s n => smul_assoc c s n)
    (fun s n1 n2 => smul_add s n1 n2) (fun c s n => smul_comm s c n))

noncomputable def actM : (M ⊗[ℤ] S) →ₗ[ℤ] M :=
  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun m s => s • m)
    (fun m1 m2 s => smul_add s m1 m2) (fun c m s => smul_comm s c m)
    (fun m s1 s2 => add_smul s1 s2 m) (fun c m s => smul_assoc c s m))

noncomputable def actRmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
  TensorProduct.map LinearMap.id (actN S N)

noncomputable def actLmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
  (TensorProduct.map (actM S M) LinearMap.id).comp (TensorProduct.assoc ℤ M S N).symm.toLinearMap

example (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) : actRmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = m ⊗ₜ (s • n) := by
  simp [actRmap, actN]
example (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) : actLmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = (s • m) ⊗ₜ n := by
  simp [actLmap, actM]

-- projection π : M ⊗[ℤ] N →+ M ⊗[S] N
noncomputable def proj : (M ⊗[ℤ] N) →+ (M ⊗[S] N) :=
  TensorProduct.liftAddHom (((TensorProduct.mk S M N).restrictScalars ℤ).toAddMonoidHom₂)
    (fun r m n => by
      have h1 : (r • m) ⊗ₜ[S] n = r • (m ⊗ₜ[S] n) := by rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul']
      have h2 : m ⊗ₜ[S] (r • n) = r • (m ⊗ₜ[S] n) := by rw [TensorProduct.tmul_smul]
      simp only [LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom₂_apply, LinearMap.coe_restrictScalars,
        TensorProduct.mk_apply]
      rw [h1, h2])

#check @proj
example (m : M) (n : N) : proj S M N (m ⊗ₜ n) = m ⊗ₜ[S] n := by
  simp [proj]

import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Map
import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Associator
import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.Grp.Basic

open TensorProduct

variable (S : Type) [CommRing S] (M N : Type)
  [AddCommGroup M] [Module S M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module S N]

noncomputable def actN : (S ⊗[ℤ] N) →ₗ[ℤ] N :=
  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun s n => s • n)
    (fun s1 s2 n => add_smul s1 s2 n) (fun c s n => smul_assoc c s n)
    (fun s n1 n2 => smul_add s n1 n2) (fun c s n => smul_comm s c n))

noncomputable def actM : (M ⊗[ℤ] S) →ₗ[ℤ] M :=
  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun m s => s • m)
    (fun m1 m2 s => smul_add s m1 m2) (fun c m s => smul_comm s c m)
    (fun m s1 s2 => add_smul s1 s2 m) (fun c m s => smul_assoc c s m))

noncomputable def actRmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
  TensorProduct.map LinearMap.id (actN S N)

noncomputable def actLmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
  (TensorProduct.map (actM S M) LinearMap.id).comp (TensorProduct.assoc ℤ M S N).symm.toLinearMap

example (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) : actRmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = m ⊗ₜ (s • n) := by
  simp [actRmap, actN, TensorProduct.map_tmul, TensorProduct.lift.tmul]
example (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) : actLmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = (s • m) ⊗ₜ n := by
  simp [actLmap, actM, TensorProduct.map_tmul, TensorProduct.lift.tmul]

noncomputable def proj : (M ⊗[ℤ] N) →+ (M ⊗[S] N) :=
  TensorProduct.liftAddHom
    { toFun := fun m => (LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom (((TensorProduct.mk S M N) m).restrictScalars ℤ))
      map_zero' := by ext n; simp
      map_add' := fun m1 m2 => by ext n; simp [TensorProduct.add_tmul] }
    (fun r m n => by simpa using (TensorProduct.smul_tmul r m n).symm ▸ rfl)

example (m : M) (n : N) : proj S M N (m ⊗ₜ n) = m ⊗ₜ[S] n := by
  simp [proj, TensorProduct.liftAddHom_tmul]

import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Map
import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Associator

open TensorProduct
variable (S : Type) [CommRing S] (M N : Type)
  [AddCommGroup M] [Module S M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module S N]

noncomputable def actN : (S ⊗[ℤ] N) →ₗ[ℤ] N :=
  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun s n => s • n)
    (fun s1 s2 n => add_smul s1 s2 n) (fun c s n => smul_assoc c s n)
    (fun s n1 n2 => smul_add s n1 n2) (fun c s n => smul_comm s c n))
noncomputable def actM : (M ⊗[ℤ] S) →ₗ[ℤ] M :=
  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun m s => s • m)
    (fun m1 m2 s => smul_add s m1 m2) (fun c m s => smul_comm s c m)
    (fun m s1 s2 => add_smul s1 s2 m) (fun c m s => smul_assoc c s m))
noncomputable def actRmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
  TensorProduct.map LinearMap.id (actN S N)
noncomputable def actLmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
  (TensorProduct.map (actM S M) LinearMap.id).comp (TensorProduct.assoc ℤ M S N).symm.toLinearMap

@[simp] lemma actN_tmul (s : S) (n : N) : actN S N (s ⊗ₜ n) = s • n := by
  rw [actN, TensorProduct.lift.tmul]; rfl
@[simp] lemma actM_tmul (m : M) (s : S) : actM S M (m ⊗ₜ s) = s • m := by
  rw [actM, TensorProduct.lift.tmul]; rfl
@[simp] lemma actRmap_tmul (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) :
    actRmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = m ⊗ₜ (s • n) := by
  rw [actRmap, TensorProduct.map_tmul, actN_tmul]; rfl
@[simp] lemma actLmap_tmul (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) :

  rw [actLmap, LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearEquiv.coe_toLinearMap,
    TensorProduct.assoc_symm_tmul, TensorProduct.map_tmul, actM_tmul]; rfl

import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Map
import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Associator
open TensorProduct
variable (S : Type) [CommRing S] (M N : Type)
  [AddCommGroup M] [Module S M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module S N]
noncomputable def actN : (S ⊗[ℤ] N) →ₗ[ℤ] N :=
  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun s n => s • n)
    (fun s1 s2 n => add_smul s1 s2 n) (fun c s n => smul_assoc c s n)
    (fun s n1 n2 => smul_add s n1 n2) (fun c s n => smul_comm s c n))
noncomputable def actM : (M ⊗[ℤ] S) →ₗ[ℤ] M :=
  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun m s => s • m)
    (fun m1 m2 s => smul_add s m1 m2) (fun c m s => smul_comm s c m)
    (fun m s1 s2 => add_smul s1 s2 m) (fun c m s => smul_assoc c s m))
noncomputable def actRmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
  TensorProduct.map LinearMap.id (actN S N)
noncomputable def actLmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
  (TensorProduct.map (actM S M) LinearMap.id).comp (TensorProduct.assoc ℤ M S N).symm.toLinearMap
@[simp] lemma actRmap_tmul (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) :
    actRmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = m ⊗ₜ (s • n) := rfl
@[simp] lemma actLmap_tmul (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) :
    actLmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = (s • m) ⊗ₜ n := rfl
noncomputable def proj : (M ⊗[ℤ] N) →+ (M ⊗[S] N) :=
  TensorProduct.liftAddHom
    { toFun := fun m => (LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom (((TensorProduct.mk S M N) m).restrictScalars ℤ))
      map_zero' := by ext n; simp
      map_add' := fun m1 m2 => by ext n; simp [TensorProduct.add_tmul] }
    (fun r m n => by simpa using (TensorProduct.smul_tmul r m n).symm ▸ rfl)
@[simp] lemma proj_tmul (m : M) (n : N) : proj S M N (m ⊗ₜ n) = m ⊗ₜ[S] n := rfl
-- coequalizing: proj ∘ actL = proj ∘ actR
example : (proj S M N).comp (actLmap S M N).toAddMonoidHom
        = (proj S M N).comp (actRmap S M N).toAddMonoidHom := by
  ext x
  · simp only [AddMonoidHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe,
      actLmap_tmul, actRmap_tmul, proj_tmul]
    exact (TensorProduct.smul_tmul _ _ _).symm

import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Map
import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Associator
open TensorProduct
variable (S : Type) [CommRing S] (M N : Type)
  [AddCommGroup M] [Module S M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module S N]
noncomputable def actN : (S ⊗[ℤ] N) →ₗ[ℤ] N :=
  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun s n => s • n)
    (fun s1 s2 n => add_smul s1 s2 n) (fun c s n => smul_assoc c s n)
    (fun s n1 n2 => smul_add s n1 n2) (fun c s n => smul_comm s c n))
noncomputable def actM : (M ⊗[ℤ] S) →ₗ[ℤ] M :=
  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun m s => s • m)
    (fun m1 m2 s => smul_add s m1 m2) (fun c m s => smul_comm s c m)
    (fun m s1 s2 => add_smul s1 s2 m) (fun c m s => smul_assoc c s m))
noncomputable def actRmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
  TensorProduct.map LinearMap.id (actN S N)
noncomputable def actLmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
  (TensorProduct.map (actM S M) LinearMap.id).comp (TensorProduct.assoc ℤ M S N).symm.toLinearMap
@[simp] lemma actRmap_tmul (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) :
    actRmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = m ⊗ₜ (s • n) := rfl
@[simp] lemma actLmap_tmul (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) :
    actLmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = (s • m) ⊗ₜ n := rfl
noncomputable def projL : (M ⊗[ℤ] N) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[S] N) :=
  (TensorProduct.liftAddHom
    { toFun := fun m => (LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom (((TensorProduct.mk S M N) m).restrictScalars ℤ))
      map_zero' := by ext n; simp
      map_add' := fun m1 m2 => by ext n; simp [TensorProduct.add_tmul] }
    (fun r m n => by simp [TensorProduct.smul_tmul])).toIntLinearMap
@[simp] lemma projL_tmul (m : M) (n : N) : projL S M N (m ⊗ₜ n) = m ⊗ₜ[S] n := rfl
-- coequalizing as linear maps
example : (projL S M N).comp (actLmap S M N) = (projL S M N).comp (actRmap S M N) := by
  ext m s n
  simp only [LinearMap.compr₂_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply, actLmap_tmul, actRmap_tmul, projL_tmul]
  exact (TensorProduct.smul_tmul _ _ _).symm

import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Map
import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Associator
import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.Grp.Basic
import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.Equalizers
open TensorProduct CategoryTheory Limits
variable (S : Type) [CommRing S] (M N : Type)
  [AddCommGroup M] [Module S M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module S N]
noncomputable def actN : (S ⊗[ℤ] N) →ₗ[ℤ] N :=
  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun s n => s • n)
    (fun s1 s2 n => add_smul s1 s2 n) (fun c s n => smul_assoc c s n)
    (fun s n1 n2 => smul_add s n1 n2) (fun c s n => smul_comm s c n))
noncomputable def actM : (M ⊗[ℤ] S) →ₗ[ℤ] M :=
  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun m s => s • m)
    (fun m1 m2 s => smul_add s m1 m2) (fun c m s => smul_comm s c m)
    (fun m s1 s2 => add_smul s1 s2 m) (fun c m s => smul_assoc c s m))
noncomputable def actRmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
  TensorProduct.map LinearMap.id (actN S N)
noncomputable def actLmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
  (TensorProduct.map (actM S M) LinearMap.id).comp (TensorProduct.assoc ℤ M S N).symm.toLinearMap
@[simp] lemma actRmap_tmul (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) : actRmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = m ⊗ₜ (s • n) := rfl
@[simp] lemma actLmap_tmul (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) : actLmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = (s • m) ⊗ₜ n := rfl
noncomputable def projL : (M ⊗[ℤ] N) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[S] N) :=
  (TensorProduct.liftAddHom
    { toFun := fun m => (LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom (((TensorProduct.mk S M N) m).restrictScalars ℤ))
      map_zero' := by ext n; simp
      map_add' := fun m1 m2 => by ext n; simp [TensorProduct.add_tmul] }
    (fun r m n => by simp [TensorProduct.smul_tmul])).toIntLinearMap
@[simp] lemma projL_tmul (m : M) (n : N) : projL S M N (m ⊗ₜ n) = m ⊗ₜ[S] n := rfl

-- AddCommGrp objects/morphisms
noncomputable def Dobj : AddCommGrp := AddCommGrp.of (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N))
noncomputable def Bobj : AddCommGrp := AddCommGrp.of (M ⊗[ℤ] N)
noncomputable def Tobj : AddCommGrp := AddCommGrp.of (M ⊗[S] N)
noncomputable def aL : Dobj S M N ⟶ Bobj S M N := AddCommGrp.ofHom (actLmap S M N).toAddMonoidHom
noncomputable def aR : Dobj S M N ⟶ Bobj S M N := AddCommGrp.ofHom (actRmap S M N).toAddMonoidHom
noncomputable def piMor : Bobj S M N ⟶ Tobj S M N := AddCommGrp.ofHom (projL S M N).toAddMonoidHom

lemma coeq : aL S M N ≫ piMor S M N = aR S M N ≫ piMor S M N := by
  ext x
  refine TensorProduct.induction_on x ?_ ?_ ?_
  · simp
  · intro m y
    refine TensorProduct.induction_on y ?_ ?_ ?_
    · simp
    · intro s n
      simp only [aL, aR, piMor, AddCommGrp.ofHom, AddCommGrp.hom_comp, Dobj, Bobj, Tobj]
      change projL S M N (actLmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n))) = projL S M N (actRmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)))
      rw [actLmap_tmul, actRmap_tmul, projL_tmul, projL_tmul, TensorProduct.smul_tmul', TensorProduct.tmul_smul]
    · intro a b ha hb; simp only [map_add, ha, hb]
  · intro a b ha hb; simp only [map_add, ha, hb]
#check @coeq

233	*definitionally* the abelian sheafification unit `toSheafify J P.presheaf`

238	`isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`. -/
239	lemma localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit (P : X.PresheafOfModules) :

241	      ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).unit.app P)) := by

253	      ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).unit.app P)) :=

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — relative tensor product as a coequalizer

This section builds the **objectwise** content of `lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`
(`relativeTensorCoequalizerIso`): over a commutative ring `S` and `S`-modules `M, N`,
the relative tensor product `M ⊗[S] N` is the coequalizer, *in the category of abelian
groups*, of the two `S`-action maps

  `M ⊗[ℤ] S ⊗[ℤ] N  ⇉  M ⊗[ℤ] N`,    `m ⊗ s ⊗ n ↦ (s • m) ⊗ n`  /  `m ⊗ (s • n)`.

This is the Mathlib-absent brick on which the strong-monoidality comparison
`isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit` rests: the underlying abelian presheaf of the
presheaf-level relative tensor `P ⊗_p Q` is, objectwise, exactly this coequalizer.  The
universal property is the abelian-group universal property of the relative tensor product,
packaged by `TensorProduct.liftAddHom`.  Everything here is axiom-clean.

The promotion of this objectwise colimit to the presheaf category `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrp`
(where colimits are computed objectwise) and the identification of the whiskered unit
`η_P ▷ Q` with the induced map of coequalizers are the next steps; see the handoff note. -/

namespace RelativeTensorCoequalizer

open TensorProduct

variable (S : Type u) [CommRing S] (M N : Type u)
  [AddCommGroup M] [Module S M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module S N]

/-- The `S`-action map `S ⊗[ℤ] N → N`, `s ⊗ n ↦ s • n`, as a `ℤ`-linear map. -/
noncomputable def actN : (S ⊗[ℤ] N) →ₗ[ℤ] N :=
  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun s n => s • n)
    (fun s1 s2 n => add_smul s1 s2 n) (fun c s n => smul_assoc c s n)
    (fun s n1 n2 => smul_add s n1 n2) (fun c s n => smul_comm s c n))

/-- The `S`-action map `M ⊗[ℤ] S → M`, `m ⊗ s ↦ s • m`, as a `ℤ`-linear map. -/
noncomputable def actM : (M ⊗[ℤ] S) →ₗ[ℤ] M :=
  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun m s => s • m)
    (fun m1 m2 s => smul_add s m1 m2) (fun c m s => smul_comm s c m)
    (fun m s1 s2 => add_smul s1 s2 m) (fun c m s => smul_assoc c s m))

/-- Right action map `M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N) → M ⊗[ℤ] N`, `m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ m ⊗ (s • n)`. -/
noncomputable def actRmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
  TensorProduct.map LinearMap.id (actN S N)

/-- Left action map `M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N) → M ⊗[ℤ] N`, `m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ (s • m) ⊗ n`. -/
noncomputable def actLmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
  (TensorProduct.map (actM S M) LinearMap.id).comp
    (TensorProduct.assoc ℤ M S N).symm.toLinearMap

@[simp] lemma actRmap_tmul (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) :
    actRmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = m ⊗ₜ (s • n) := rfl

@[simp] lemma actLmap_tmul (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) :
    actLmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = (s • m) ⊗ₜ n := rfl

/-- The canonical projection `M ⊗[ℤ] N → M ⊗[S] N`, `m ⊗ n ↦ m ⊗ n`, as a `ℤ`-linear
map.  It is the cofork map exhibiting `M ⊗[S] N` as the coequalizer. -/
noncomputable def projL : (M ⊗[ℤ] N) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[S] N) :=
  (TensorProduct.liftAddHom
    { toFun := fun m =>
        (LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom (((TensorProduct.mk S M N) m).restrictScalars ℤ))
      map_zero' := by ext n; simp
      map_add' := fun m1 m2 => by ext n; simp [TensorProduct.add_tmul] }
    (fun r m n => by simp [TensorProduct.smul_tmul])).toIntLinearMap

@[simp] lemma projL_tmul (m : M) (n : N) : projL S M N (m ⊗ₜ n) = m ⊗ₜ[S] n := rfl

/-- The domain object `M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)` of the parallel pair. -/
noncomputable def Dobj : AddCommGrp.{u} := AddCommGrp.of (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N))
/-- The middle object `M ⊗[ℤ] N` of the parallel pair. -/
noncomputable def Bobj : AddCommGrp.{u} := AddCommGrp.of (M ⊗[ℤ] N)
/-- The coequalizer apex `M ⊗[S] N`. -/
noncomputable def Tobj : AddCommGrp.{u} := AddCommGrp.of (M ⊗[S] N)

/-- Left action map as a morphism of abelian groups. -/
noncomputable def aL : Dobj S M N ⟶ Bobj S M N := AddCommGrp.ofHom (actLmap S M N).toAddMonoidHom
/-- Right action map as a morphism of abelian groups. -/
noncomputable def aR : Dobj S M N ⟶ Bobj S M N := AddCommGrp.ofHom (actRmap S M N).toAddMonoidHom
/-- The projection as a morphism of abelian groups. -/
noncomputable def piMor : Bobj S M N ⟶ Tobj S M N := AddCommGrp.ofHom (projL S M N).toAddMonoidHom

/-- The projection coequalizes the two action maps. -/
lemma coeq_condition : aL S M N ≫ piMor S M N = aR S M N ≫ piMor S M N := by
  ext x
  refine TensorProduct.induction_on x ?_ ?_ ?_
  · simp
  · intro m y
    refine TensorProduct.induction_on y ?_ ?_ ?_
    · simp
    · intro s n
      change projL S M N (actLmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)))
        = projL S M N (actRmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)))
      rw [actLmap_tmul, actRmap_tmul, projL_tmul, projL_tmul,
        TensorProduct.smul_tmul', TensorProduct.tmul_smul]
    · intro a b ha hb
      rw [map_add, map_add] at *
      simp only [map_add, ha, hb]
  · intro a b ha hb
    simp only [map_add, ha, hb]

/-- The cofork `M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N) ⇉ M ⊗[ℤ] N → M ⊗[S] N` of abelian groups. -/
noncomputable def cofork : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N) :=
  Limits.Cofork.ofπ (piMor S M N) (coeq_condition S M N)

import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Map
import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Associator
import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Sheafification
import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.Equalizers
open TensorProduct CategoryTheory
variable (S : Type) [CommRing S] (M N : Type)
  [AddCommGroup M] [Module S M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module S N]
noncomputable def actN : (S ⊗[ℤ] N) →ₗ[ℤ] N :=
  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun s n => s • n)
    (fun s1 s2 n => add_smul s1 s2 n) (fun c s n => smul_assoc c s n)
    (fun s n1 n2 => smul_add s n1 n2) (fun c s n => smul_comm s c n))
noncomputable def actM : (M ⊗[ℤ] S) →ₗ[ℤ] M :=
  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun m s => s • m)
    (fun m1 m2 s => smul_add s m1 m2) (fun c m s => smul_comm s c m)
    (fun m s1 s2 => add_smul s1 s2 m) (fun c m s => smul_assoc c s m))
noncomputable def actRmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) := TensorProduct.map LinearMap.id (actN S N)
noncomputable def actLmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
  (TensorProduct.map (actM S M) LinearMap.id).comp (TensorProduct.assoc ℤ M S N).symm.toLinearMap
@[simp] lemma actRmap_tmul (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) : actRmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = m ⊗ₜ (s • n) := rfl
@[simp] lemma actLmap_tmul (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) : actLmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = (s • m) ⊗ₜ n := rfl
noncomputable def projL : (M ⊗[ℤ] N) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[S] N) :=
  (TensorProduct.liftAddHom
    { toFun := fun m => (LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom (((TensorProduct.mk S M N) m).restrictScalars ℤ))
      map_zero' := by ext n; simp
      map_add' := fun m1 m2 => by ext n; simp [TensorProduct.add_tmul] }
    (fun r m n => by simp [TensorProduct.smul_tmul])).toIntLinearMap
@[simp] lemma projL_tmul (m : M) (n : N) : projL S M N (m ⊗ₜ n) = m ⊗ₜ[S] n := rfl

lemma projL_comp_act : (projL S M N).comp (actLmap S M N) = (projL S M N).comp (actRmap S M N) := by
  apply TensorProduct.ext'
  intro m x
  induction x with
  | zero => simp
  | tmul s n =>
    simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply, actLmap_tmul, actRmap_tmul, projL_tmul]
    rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul', TensorProduct.tmul_smul]
  | add a b ha hb => rw [tmul_add, map_add, map_add, ha, hb]

noncomputable def Dobj : AddCommGrpCat := AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N))
noncomputable def Bobj : AddCommGrpCat := AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] N)
noncomputable def Tobj : AddCommGrpCat := AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[S] N)
noncomputable def aL : Dobj S M N ⟶ Bobj S M N := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (actLmap S M N).toAddMonoidHom
noncomputable def aR : Dobj S M N ⟶ Bobj S M N := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (actRmap S M N).toAddMonoidHom
noncomputable def piMor : Bobj S M N ⟶ Tobj S M N := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (projL S M N).toAddMonoidHom
lemma coeq_condition : aL S M N ≫ piMor S M N = aR S M N ≫ piMor S M N := by
  ext x
  exact LinearMap.congr_fun (projL_comp_act S M N) x

import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Map
import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Associator
import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Sheafification
import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.Equalizers
open TensorProduct CategoryTheory
variable (S : Type) [CommRing S] (M N : Type)
  [AddCommGroup M] [Module S M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module S N]
noncomputable def actN : (S ⊗[ℤ] N) →ₗ[ℤ] N :=
  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun s n => s • n)
    (fun s1 s2 n => add_smul s1 s2 n) (fun c s n => smul_assoc c s n)
    (fun s n1 n2 => smul_add s n1 n2) (fun c s n => smul_comm s c n))
noncomputable def actM : (M ⊗[ℤ] S) →ₗ[ℤ] M :=
  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun m s => s • m)
    (fun m1 m2 s => smul_add s m1 m2) (fun c m s => smul_comm s c m)
    (fun m s1 s2 => add_smul s1 s2 m) (fun c m s => smul_assoc c s m))
noncomputable def actRmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) := TensorProduct.map LinearMap.id (actN S N)
noncomputable def actLmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
  (TensorProduct.map (actM S M) LinearMap.id).comp (TensorProduct.assoc ℤ M S N).symm.toLinearMap
@[simp] lemma actRmap_tmul (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) : actRmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = m ⊗ₜ (s • n) := rfl
@[simp] lemma actLmap_tmul (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) : actLmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = (s • m) ⊗ₜ n := rfl
noncomputable def projL : (M ⊗[ℤ] N) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[S] N) :=
  (TensorProduct.liftAddHom
    { toFun := fun m => (LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom (((TensorProduct.mk S M N) m).restrictScalars ℤ))
      map_zero' := by ext n; simp
      map_add' := fun m1 m2 => by ext n; simp [TensorProduct.add_tmul] }
    (fun r m n => by simp [TensorProduct.smul_tmul])).toIntLinearMap
@[simp] lemma projL_tmul (m : M) (n : N) : projL S M N (m ⊗ₜ n) = m ⊗ₜ[S] n := rfl
lemma projL_comp_act : (projL S M N).comp (actLmap S M N) = (projL S M N).comp (actRmap S M N) := by
  apply TensorProduct.ext'
  intro m x
  induction x with
  | zero => rw [tmul_zero, map_zero, map_zero]
  | tmul s n =>
    simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply, actLmap_tmul, actRmap_tmul, projL_tmul]
    rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul', TensorProduct.tmul_smul]
  | add a b ha hb => rw [tmul_add, map_add, map_add, ha, hb]

noncomputable def aL :
    AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) ⟶ AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (actLmap S M N).toAddMonoidHom
noncomputable def aR :
    AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) ⟶ AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (actRmap S M N).toAddMonoidHom
noncomputable def piMor :
    AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] N) ⟶ AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[S] N) :=
  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (projL S M N).toAddMonoidHom
lemma coeq_condition : aL S M N ≫ piMor S M N = aR S M N ≫ piMor S M N := by
  ext x
  exact LinearMap.congr_fun (projL_comp_act S M N) x
noncomputable def cofork : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N) :=
  Limits.Cofork.ofπ (piMor S M N) (coeq_condition S M N)
#check @cofork

  rw [h]
example (A B C : AddCommGrpCat) (f : A ⟶ B) (g : B ⟶ C) (x : A) : (f ≫ g) x = g (f x) := by
  rw [comp_apply]

import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Map
import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Associator
import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Sheafification
import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.Equalizers
open TensorProduct CategoryTheory Limits
variable (S : Type) [CommRing S] (M N : Type)
  [AddCommGroup M] [Module S M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module S N]
noncomputable def actN : (S ⊗[ℤ] N) →ₗ[ℤ] N :=
  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun s n => s • n)
    (fun s1 s2 n => add_smul s1 s2 n) (fun c s n => smul_assoc c s n)
    (fun s n1 n2 => smul_add s n1 n2) (fun c s n => smul_comm s c n))
noncomputable def actM : (M ⊗[ℤ] S) →ₗ[ℤ] M :=
  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun m s => s • m)
    (fun m1 m2 s => smul_add s m1 m2) (fun c m s => smul_comm s c m)
    (fun m s1 s2 => add_smul s1 s2 m) (fun c m s => smul_assoc c s m))
noncomputable def actRmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) := TensorProduct.map LinearMap.id (actN S N)
noncomputable def actLmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
  (TensorProduct.map (actM S M) LinearMap.id).comp (TensorProduct.assoc ℤ M S N).symm.toLinearMap
@[simp] lemma actRmap_tmul (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) : actRmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = m ⊗ₜ (s • n) := rfl
@[simp] lemma actLmap_tmul (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) : actLmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = (s • m) ⊗ₜ n := rfl
noncomputable def projL : (M ⊗[ℤ] N) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[S] N) :=
  (TensorProduct.liftAddHom
    { toFun := fun m => (LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom (((TensorProduct.mk S M N) m).restrictScalars ℤ))
      map_zero' := by ext n; simp
      map_add' := fun m1 m2 => by ext n; simp [TensorProduct.add_tmul] }
    (fun r m n => by simp [TensorProduct.smul_tmul])).toIntLinearMap
@[simp] lemma projL_tmul (m : M) (n : N) : projL S M N (m ⊗ₜ n) = m ⊗ₜ[S] n := rfl
lemma projL_comp_act : (projL S M N).comp (actLmap S M N) = (projL S M N).comp (actRmap S M N) := by
  apply TensorProduct.ext'
  intro m x
  induction x with
  | zero => rw [tmul_zero, map_zero, map_zero]
  | tmul s n =>
    show projL S M N (actLmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n))) = projL S M N (actRmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)))
    rw [actLmap_tmul, actRmap_tmul, projL_tmul, projL_tmul, TensorProduct.smul_tmul', TensorProduct.tmul_smul]
  | add a b ha hb => rw [tmul_add, map_add, map_add, ha, hb]
noncomputable def aL :
    AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) ⟶ AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (actLmap S M N).toAddMonoidHom
noncomputable def aR :
    AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) ⟶ AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (actRmap S M N).toAddMonoidHom
noncomputable def piMor :
    AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] N) ⟶ AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[S] N) :=
  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (projL S M N).toAddMonoidHom
@[simp] lemma aL_apply (x) : (ConcreteCategory.hom (aL S M N)) x = actLmap S M N x := rfl
@[simp] lemma aR_apply (x) : (ConcreteCategory.hom (aR S M N)) x = actRmap S M N x := rfl
@[simp] lemma piMor_apply (x) : (ConcreteCategory.hom (piMor S M N)) x = projL S M N x := rfl
lemma coeq_condition : aL S M N ≫ piMor S M N = aR S M N ≫ piMor S M N := by
  ext x; exact LinearMap.congr_fun (projL_comp_act S M N) x
noncomputable def cofork : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N) :=
  Limits.Cofork.ofπ (piMor S M N) (coeq_condition S M N)

-- desc for a cofork s
noncomputable def descHom (s : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N)) :
    (M ⊗[S] N) →+ s.pt :=
  TensorProduct.liftAddHom
    { toFun := fun m =>
        { toFun := fun n => (ConcreteCategory.hom s.π) (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n)
          map_zero' := by rw [tmul_zero, map_zero]
          map_add' := fun n1 n2 => by rw [tmul_add, map_add] }
      map_zero' := by ext n; simp [zero_tmul]
      map_add' := fun m1 m2 => by ext n; simp [add_tmul] }
    (fun a m n => by
      simp only [AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
      have h := s.condition
      have key := congrArg (fun φ => (ConcreteCategory.hom φ) (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] (a ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n))) h
      simpa using key)
#check @descHom

  | add a b ha hb => rw [tmul_add, map_add, map_add, ha, hb]
noncomputable def aL : AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) ⟶ AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (actLmap S M N).toAddMonoidHom
noncomputable def aR : AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) ⟶ AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (actRmap S M N).toAddMonoidHom
noncomputable def piMor : AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] N) ⟶ AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[S] N) :=
  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (projL S M N).toAddMonoidHom
@[simp] lemma aL_apply (x) : (ConcreteCategory.hom (aL S M N)) x = actLmap S M N x := rfl
@[simp] lemma aR_apply (x) : (ConcreteCategory.hom (aR S M N)) x = actRmap S M N x := rfl
@[simp] lemma piMor_apply (x) : (ConcreteCategory.hom (piMor S M N)) x = projL S M N x := rfl
lemma coeq_condition : aL S M N ≫ piMor S M N = aR S M N ≫ piMor S M N := by
  ext x; exact LinearMap.congr_fun (projL_comp_act S M N) x
noncomputable def cofork : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N) :=
  Limits.Cofork.ofπ (piMor S M N) (coeq_condition S M N)
noncomputable def descHom (s : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N)) : (M ⊗[S] N) →+ s.pt :=
  TensorProduct.liftAddHom
    { toFun := fun m =>
        { toFun := fun n => (ConcreteCategory.hom s.π) (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n)
          map_zero' := by rw [tmul_zero, map_zero]
          map_add' := fun n1 n2 => by rw [tmul_add, map_add] }
      map_zero' := by ext n; simp [zero_tmul]
      map_add' := fun m1 m2 => by ext n; simp [add_tmul] }
    (fun a m n => by
      simp only [AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
      have key := congrArg (fun φ => (ConcreteCategory.hom φ) (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] (a ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n))) s.condition
      simpa using key)
@[simp] lemma descHom_tmul (s : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N)) (m : M) (n : N) :
    descHom S M N s (m ⊗ₜ[S] n) = (ConcreteCategory.hom s.π) (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n) := rfl

noncomputable def isColimitCofork : Limits.IsColimit (cofork S M N) :=
  Limits.Cofork.IsColimit.mk _
    (fun s => AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (descHom S M N s))
    (fun s => by
      ext x
      induction x with
      | zero => simp
      | tmul m n => simp [cofork]
      | add a b ha hb => simp only [map_add, ha, hb])
    (fun s f hf => by
      ext x
      induction x with
      | zero => simp
      | tmul m n =>
        have hfx := congrArg (fun φ => (ConcreteCategory.hom φ) (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n)) hf
        simp only [cofork] at hfx ⊢
        simpa using hfx
      | add a b ha hb => simp only [map_add, ha, hb])
#check @isColimitCofork
